item.title: Django 第 3 部分 - 使用 dj-rest-auth 和 allauth 进行用户身份验证 | Django Part 3 - User authentication with dj-rest-auth and allauth
item.description:
对于本教程的这一部分，我们将使用 dj-rest-auth 和 allauth 包来帮助我们创建用于帐户注册、登录和电子邮件验证的 API 端点。 使用 Django 实现用户身份验证有多种不同的方法，将使用令牌身份验证。
我们还需要安装 Django rest 框架包。 当我们在安装包时，我们也应该得到 django cors headers 包，作为最佳安全实践，我们应该防止 CORS 攻击。

像往常一样，使用 pip 安装软件包。

 py -m pip install dj-rest-auth django-allauth djangorestframework django-cors-headers

安装包后，我们需要更新项目设置。 将以下内容添加到 backend/settings.py 中的 INSTALLED_APPS 列表中。

 INSTALLED_APPS = [ ... 'django.contrib.sites', 'corsheaders', 'rest_framework', 'rest_framework.authtoken', 'allauth', 'allauth.account', 'allauth.socialaccount', 'dj_rest_auth', 'dj_rest_auth.registration', ... ]

现在我们需要运行一些数据库迁移，因为新安装的包使用了一些新的数据库模型。

将 cors 中间件添加到 MIDDLEWARE 列表。 它必须在通用中间件之前。

 MIDDLEWARE = [ ... 'corsheaders.middleware.CorsMiddleware', ... ]

将所有这些设置添加到 settings.py 的底部。

 REST_FRAMEWORK = { "DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES": [ "rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny", ], "DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES": [ "rest_framework.authentication.SessionAuthentication", "rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication", ], } AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS = ( "django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend", "allauth.account.auth_backends.AuthenticationBackend" ) CORS_ORIGIN_WHITELIST = ( "http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:8000", ) CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = ["http://localhost:3000"] SITE_ID = 1 EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.console.EmailBackend' ACCOUNT_AUTHENTICATION_METHOD = 'email' ACCOUNT_USERNAME_REQUIRED = True ACCOUNT_EMAIL_REQUIRED = True ACCOUNT_UNIQUE_EMAIL = True ACCOUNT_CONFIRM_EMAIL_ON_GET = True ACCOUNT_EMAIL_CONFIRMATION_AUTHENTICATED_REDIRECT_URL = 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1/dj-rest-auth/login/' LOGIN_URL = 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1/dj-rest-auth/login/'

目前，我们只是使用控制台来接收电子邮件验证电子邮件。 如果您想使用真实的电子邮件，您可以通过将 EMAIL_BACKEND 设置替换为以下内容来使用 Gmail：

 EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.smtp.EmailBackend' EMAIL_USE_TLS = True EMAIL_HOST = 'smtp.gmail.com' EMAIL_HOST_USER = os.environ.get("EMAIL_USER") EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD = os.environ.get("EMAIL_PASSWORD") EMAIL_PORT = 587

最佳做法是使用 .env 文件来存储您的电子邮件和密码。 安装加载 dotenv 包以使用 .env 文件。

 py -m pip install python-dotenv

现在在与您的设置文件相同的目录中创建一个 .env 。 在 .env 文件中，写下您的电子邮件和密码。

 EMAIL_USER=”YOUR EMAIL" EMAIL_PASSWORD="YOUR PASSWORD"

将以下代码添加到 settings.py 文件的顶部。

 from dotenv import load_dotenv import os load_dotenv()

如果您尝试使用常规电子邮件密码，您很可能会收到错误消息，因为您没有获得 Google 的许可。 您将需要创建一个应用程序密码。 您可以在https://support.google.com/mail/answer/185833?hl=en找到有关如何执行此操作的说明

现在我们需要创建 URL 来访问我们安装的包提供的端点。

在 backend/urls.py 添加以下内容：

 from django.contrib import admin from django.urls import path, include, re_path from dj_rest_auth.registration.views import VerifyEmailView, ConfirmEmailView urlpatterns = [ path('admin/', admin.site.urls), path('api/v1/dj-rest-auth/', include('dj_rest_auth.urls')), path( 'api/v1/dj-rest-auth/registration/account-confirm-email/<str:key>/', ConfirmEmailView.as_view(), ), # Needs to be defined before the registration path path('api/v1/dj-rest-auth/registration/', include('dj_rest_auth.registration.urls')), path( 'api/v1/dj-rest-auth/account-confirm-email/', VerifyEmailView.as_view(), name='account_email_verification_sent' ), ]

接下来，我们将为 /user 端点创建一个序列化程序。 序列化器还提供反序列化，允许在首先验证传入数据后将解析的数据转换回复杂类型。

在用户目录中，创建一个名为 serializers.py 的新文件。 在 serializers.py 中：

 from rest_framework import serializers from .models import CustomUser class UserProfileSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer): class Meta: model = CustomUser fields = ('pk', 'email', 'username', 'email_verified')

现在我们需要通过将序列化程序添加到 settings.py 文件来使用它。 在 settings.py 的底部：

 REST_AUTH_SERIALIZERS = { 'USER_DETAILS_SERIALIZER': 'users.serializers.UserProfileSerializer' }

我们终于添加了我们需要的一切。 导航到http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/dj-rest-auth/registration/并尝试创建一个新帐户。

输入用户名、电子邮件和密码。 您应该会收到一个响应，其中包含将用于经过身份验证的 API 请求的身份验证密钥。 您还应该收到一封电子邮件以验证您的电子邮件地址。 这将在您的电子邮件收件箱或控制台中，具体取决于您之前的设置。 现在，点击电子邮件中的链接将更改一个单独的数据库模型，而不是我们自定义用户模型中的 email_verified 字段。

我们希望能够更新我们的用户模型，所以我们需要使用信号。 当用户验证他们的电子邮件时，这将发送一个信号。 在 users 目录中创建一个 signals.py 文件。 在信号.py 中：



原文:

For this part of the tutorial, we will be using the dj-rest-auth and allauth packages to help us create API endpoints for account registration, login and email verification. There are various different ways to implement user authentication with Django, will be using token authentication.
We also need to install the Django rest framework package. While we are at it installing packages, we should get the django cors headers package too, as for best security practices we should be protecting against CORS attacks.

As usual, use pip to install the packages.

py -m  pip install dj-rest-auth django-allauth djangorestframework django-cors-headers

After installing the packages, we need to update the project settings. Add the following to the INSTALLED_APPS list in backend/settings.py.

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    ...
    'django.contrib.sites',
    'corsheaders',
    'rest_framework',
    'rest_framework.authtoken',
    'allauth',
    'allauth.account',
    'allauth.socialaccount',
    'dj_rest_auth',
    'dj_rest_auth.registration',
    ...
]

Now we need to run some database migrations as the newly installed packages use some new database models.

Add the cors middleware to the MIDDLEWARE list. It must be before the common middleware.

MIDDLEWARE = [
    ...
    'corsheaders.middleware.CorsMiddleware',
    ...
]

Add all of these settings to the bottom of settings.py.

REST_FRAMEWORK = {
    "DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES": [
        "rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny",
    ],
    "DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES": [
        "rest_framework.authentication.SessionAuthentication",
        "rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication",
    ],
}

AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS = (
   "django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend",
   "allauth.account.auth_backends.AuthenticationBackend"
)

CORS_ORIGIN_WHITELIST = (
    "http://localhost:3000",
    "http://localhost:8000",
)

CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = ["http://localhost:3000"]

SITE_ID = 1

EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.console.EmailBackend'
ACCOUNT_AUTHENTICATION_METHOD = 'email'
ACCOUNT_USERNAME_REQUIRED = True
ACCOUNT_EMAIL_REQUIRED = True
ACCOUNT_UNIQUE_EMAIL = True

ACCOUNT_CONFIRM_EMAIL_ON_GET = True
ACCOUNT_EMAIL_CONFIRMATION_AUTHENTICATED_REDIRECT_URL = 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1/dj-rest-auth/login/'
LOGIN_URL = 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1/dj-rest-auth/login/'

For now, we are just using the console to receive the email verification email. If you want to use a real email, you can use Gmail by replacing the EMAIL_BACKEND setting with the following:

EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.smtp.EmailBackend'
EMAIL_USE_TLS = True
EMAIL_HOST = 'smtp.gmail.com'
EMAIL_HOST_USER = os.environ.get("EMAIL_USER")
EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD = os.environ.get("EMAIL_PASSWORD")
EMAIL_PORT = 587

It is best practice to use a .env file to store your email and password. Install the load dotenv package to use .env files.

py -m pip install python-dotenv

Now create a .env in the same directory as your settings file. In the .env file, write your email and password.

EMAIL_USER=”YOUR EMAIL"
EMAIL_PASSWORD="YOUR PASSWORD"

Add the following code to the top of the settings.py file.

from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os

load_dotenv()

If you try to use your regular email password, you will most likely get an error as you don’t have permission from Google. You will need to create an app password. You can find instructions on how to do that at https://support.google.com/mail/answer/185833?hl=en

Now we need to create the URLs to access the endpoints provided by the packages we installed.

In backend/urls.py add the following:

from django.contrib import admin
from django.urls import path, include, re_path
from dj_rest_auth.registration.views import VerifyEmailView, ConfirmEmailView

urlpatterns = [
    path('admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path('api/v1/dj-rest-auth/', include('dj_rest_auth.urls')),
    path(
        'api/v1/dj-rest-auth/registration/account-confirm-email/<str:key>/',
        ConfirmEmailView.as_view(),
    ), # Needs to be defined before the registration path
    path('api/v1/dj-rest-auth/registration/', include('dj_rest_auth.registration.urls')),
    path(
        'api/v1/dj-rest-auth/account-confirm-email/',
        VerifyEmailView.as_view(),
        name='account_email_verification_sent'
    ),
]

Next, we will create a serializer for the /user endpoint. Serializers in Django REST Framework are responsible for converting objects into data types understandable by javascript and front-end frameworks. Serializers also provide deserialization, allowing parsed data to be converted back into complex types, after first validating the incoming data.

In the users directory, create a new file called serializers.py. In serializers.py:

from rest_framework import serializers
from .models import CustomUser

class UserProfileSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = CustomUser
        fields = ('pk', 'email', 'username', 'email_verified')

Now we need to use the serializer by adding it to the settings.py file. At the bottom of settings.py:

REST_AUTH_SERIALIZERS = {
    'USER_DETAILS_SERIALIZER': 'users.serializers.UserProfileSerializer'
}

We have finally added everything we need. Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/dj-rest-auth/registration/ and try to create a new account.

Enter a username, email and password. You should receive a response containing an authentication key that will use for authenticated API requests. You should also receive an email to verify your email address. This will either be in your email inbox or the console depending on what you setup previously. Right now, following the link in the email will change a separate database model and not the email_verified field in our custom User model.

We want to be able to update our user model, so we need to use a signal. This will send a signal when a user verifies their email. Create a signals.py file inside the users directory. In signals.py:
item.author: willp11

item.title: 如何在没有键长度和键太长的情况下处理索引键规范 | Howto handle index key specification without a key length and key was too long
item.description:
您创建数据结构并定义一个表，该表将允许 UPSERT（如果文档不存在则插入）并得到以下错误之一：

1170 BLOB/TEXT column 'document_id' used in key specification without a key length

Specified key was too long; max key length is 3072 bytes

问题

创建索引时，每个键允许的最大字节数有一个特定的实施限制。
它是字节而不是字符，因为它取决于排序规则的定义字符集，每个字符需要多少字节。

这是一个示例表，它使用列collection和document_id作为主键：

 CREATE TABLE type_varchar ( `collection` TEXT COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL , `document_id` varchar ( 255 ) COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL , `document_type` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL , `document` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL COMMENT '(DC2Type:json)' , PRIMARY KEY ( collection , document_id ) ) ENGINE = InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET = utf8mb3 COLLATE = utf8mb3_unicode_ci ;

其结果将是此错误：

SQLSTATE[42000]: Syntax error or access violation: 1170 BLOB/TEXT column 'document_id' used in key specification without a key length

网络上的某些人或消息来源会告诉您，您不应该使用 BLOB/TEXT 作为索引，并建议使用 VARCHAR：

 CREATE TABLE type_varchar ( `collection` varchar ( 800 ) COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL , `document_id` varchar ( 255 ) COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL , `document_type` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL , `document` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL COMMENT '(DC2Type:json)' , PRIMARY KEY ( collection , document_id ) ) ENGINE = InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET = utf8mb3 COLLATE = utf8mb3_unicode_ci ;

其结果将是此错误：

SQLSTATE[42000]: Syntax error or access violation: Specified key was too long; max key length is 3072 bytes

怎么修？

修复这两个错误只需要在主键中指定的列中添加一个长度值(768) ：

PRIMARY KEY (collection(768),document_id)

但是你如何计算长度的值呢？

  1. 获取您的收藏字符集COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci
  2. 查找字符utf8mb3或别名utf8的字节

基于2.错误，数据库引擎（MySQL / MariaDB）每列最多允许3072字节：
3072 / 3 = 1024

在我的 MariaDB 10.8.3 实例上，这失败了，因为看起来索引正在使用utf8mb4一个 4 字节排序规则3072 / 4 = 768有效！

乍一看，两者都支持高达 65,535 字节，并且只需要真实数据使用的空间，但 MySQL 对每行的最大大小施加了 65,535 字节的限制。 这意味着即使您的 VARCHAR 字段的“理论”最大大小为 65,535 个字符，如果您的表中有多个列，您也无法实现这一点。



原文:

You create your datastructure and define a table which will allow UPSERT (insert if document not exists) and get one of this errors:

1170 BLOB/TEXT column 'document_id' used in key specification without a key length

Specified key was too long; max key length is 3072 bytes

The Problem

When creating an index there is an impelementation specific limit on the maximum number of bytes allowd per key.
It is bytes and not characters as it depends on the defined charset of the collation how many bytes are needed per character.

Here is a sample table which is uses the columns collection and document_id for the primary key:

CREATE TABLE type_varchar ( `collection` TEXT COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `document_id` varchar(255) COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `document_type` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `document` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL COMMENT '(DC2Type:json)', PRIMARY KEY (collection,document_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb3 COLLATE=utf8mb3_unicode_ci;

The result of this will be this error:

SQLSTATE[42000]: Syntax error or access violation: 1170 BLOB/TEXT column 'document_id' used in key specification without a key length

Some people or sources on the web will tell you that you should not use BLOB/TEXT for the index and suggest to use VARCHAR:

CREATE TABLE type_varchar ( `collection` varchar(800) COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `document_id` varchar(255) COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `document_type` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `document` longtext COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci NOT NULL COMMENT '(DC2Type:json)', PRIMARY KEY (collection,document_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb3 COLLATE=utf8mb3_unicode_ci;

The result of this will be this error:

SQLSTATE[42000]: Syntax error or access violation: Specified key was too long; max key length is 3072 bytes

How To Fix?

Fixing both errors only requires to add a length value of (768) to the column specified in the primary key:

PRIMARY KEY (collection(768),document_id)

But how do you calulate the value for the length?

  1. get your collection charset COLLATE utf8mb3_unicode_ci
  2. lookup the bytes for the charaterset utf8mb3 or the alias utf8

utf8mb3: 3 bytes

Based on the 2. error, a maximum of 3072 bytes is allowed per column by the database engine (MySQL / MariaDB):
3072 / 3 = 1024

On my MariaDB 10.8.3 instance this fails as it looks like that the index is using utf8mb4 a 4 byte collation 3072 / 4 = 768 which works!

Difference between TEXT and VARCHAR

On a first look both support up to 65,535 bytes and only need the space used by real data but MySQL imposes a limit of 65,535 bytes for the max size of each row. The size of a VARCHAR column is counted towards the maximum row size, while TEXT columns are assumed to be storing their data by reference so they only need 9-12 bytes.

That means even if the "theoretical" max size of your VARCHAR field is 65,535 characters you won't be able to achieve that if you have more than one column in your table.
item.author: aheissenberger

item.title: 讨论：作为开发人员，您更愿意成为专家还是通才？ | Discuss: As a developer would you rather be a specialist or a generalist?
item.description:
大家好，

所以我一直想问哪个更好，是通才开发者还是专业开发者？

作为一名开发人员，您更愿意成为一名通才，一个对不同类型的软件有很多了解并且可以使用它们来完成工作的人吗？ 还是专家，专门研究某种软件的人？

你们有什么感想？

另外，如果您能加入我在 Twitter 上的在线开发者社区，分享您的文章、提出问题并分享您正在学习的不同内容，我将不胜感激。 这是链接



原文:

item.title: 从 Anxietycareonline 了解成人的胎儿酒精综合症 | Learn About Fetal Alcohol Syndrome In Adults From Anxietycareonline
item.description:
概述

胎儿酒精综合症是儿童在母亲怀孕期间因接触酒精而导致的病症。 胎儿酒精模式会导致脑损伤和生长问题。 由胎儿酒精模式引起的问题因孩子而异，但由胎儿酒精模式引起的枯萎病是不可逆的。

怀孕期间没有已知可以安全饮用的酒精量。 但是，如果您在怀孕期间饮酒，您会使您的宝宝​​面临胎儿酒精综合症的威胁。

不过，如果您怀疑您的孩子患有胎儿酒精综合症，请尽快与您的黄鱼交谈。 事先的意见可能有助于减少类似于学习困难和行为问题的问题。

单击此处获取完整信息

症状

胎儿酒精模式症状的不灵活性各不相同，一些孩子的传递程度远低于其他孩子。 胎儿酒精综合症的体征和症状可能包括身体虚弱、智力或认知障碍，以及日常生活中的表现和管理问题。

物理缺陷

身体疾病可能包括：

  • 独特的面部特征，包括小眼睛、特别薄的上唇、短而上翘的鼻子以及鼻子和上唇之间光滑的皮肤脸

  • 关节、树枝和油条的疤痕

  • 出生前后身体发育缓慢

  • 视力困难或冰雹问题

  • 小头围和脑容量

  • 心脏病和羽毛和骨骼问题

大脑和中枢神经系统问题

大脑和中枢神经系统的问题可能包括：

  • 协作或平衡不佳

  • 智力障碍、学习疾病和发育迟缓

  • 记忆力差

  • 注意力和回收信息方面的问题

  • 逻辑和解决问题的困难

  • 难以关联选择的后果

  • 判断力差

  • 情绪激动或多动

  • 瞬息万变的心情

什么时候去看医生？

不过，如果您怀孕并且无法停止饮酒，请向您的产科医生、初级保健黄鱼或内部健康专家寻求帮助。

因为早期意见可能有助于减少胎儿酒精模式儿童长期问题的威胁，所以让您的孩子的黄鱼知道您在怀孕期间是否饮酒。 在寻求帮助之前不要留下来等待问题出现。

尽管如此，您可能不知道自然妈妈在怀孕期间是否饮酒——如果您已经娶了一个孩子或正在提供寄养，您的孩子可能患有胎儿酒精综合症可能最初并不是对您而言的。 尽管如此，如果您的孩子在识字和动作方面有问题，请与他或她的黄鱼交谈，以便将根本原因联系起来。

继续阅读…..

`

Overview

Fetal alcohol syndrome is a condition in a child that results from alcohol exposure during the mama 's gestation. Fetal alcohol pattern causes brain damage and growth problems. The problems caused by fetal alcohol patterns vary from child to child, but blights caused by fetal alcohol patterns aren't reversible.

There's no quantity of alcohol that is known to be safe to consume during pregnancy. However, you place your baby at threat of fetal alcohol syndrome, If you drink during gestation.

Still, talk to your croaker as soon as possible, If you suspect your child has fetal alcohol syndrome. Beforehand opinion may help to reduce problems similar to learning difficulties and behavioral issues.

CLICK HERE FOR TO GET FULL INFORMATION

Symptoms

The inflexibility of fetal alcohol pattern symptoms varies, with some children passing them to a far lesser degree than others. Signs and symptoms of fetal alcohol syndrome may include any blend of physical blights, intellectual or cognitive disabilities, and problems performing and managing with diurnal life.

Physical Defects

Physical Blights may include:

  • Distinctive facial features, including small eyes, an exceptionally thin upper lip, a short, upturned nose, and a smooth skin face between the nose and upper lip 

  • Scars of joints, branches and fritters

  • Slow physical growth before and after birth 

  • Vision difficulties or hail problems 

  • Small head circumference and brain size 

  • Heart blights and problems with feathers and bones

Brain and Central Nervous System Problems

Problems with the brain and central nervous system may include:

  • Poor collaboration or balance 

  • Intellectual disability, learning diseases and delayed development 

  • Poor memory 

  • Trouble with attention and with recycling information 

  • Difficulty with logic and problem- working 

  • Difficulty relating consequences of choices 

  • Poor judgment Chops 

  • Edginess or hyperactivity 

  • fleetly changing moods

When To See A Doctor?

Still, ask your obstetrician, primary care croaker or an internal health professional for help, If you are pregnant and can not stop drinking. 

Because early opinion may help reduce the threat of long- term problems for children with fetal alcohol patterns, let your child's croaker know if you drank alcohol while you were pregnant. Do not stay for problems to arise before seeking help.

Still, you may not know if the natural mama drank alcohol while pregnant — and it may not  originally be to you that your child may have a fetal alcohol syndrome, If you have espoused a child or are furnishing foster care. Still, if your child has problems with literacy and geste, talk with his or her croaker so that the underpinning cause might be linked.

item.author: thomassjohnn01

item.title: 使用 Dokku 部署 Ghost | Deploy Ghost using Dokku
item.description:
我开始使用Ghost作为我个人网站的无头 cms，它的作用就像一个魅力，但我不得不花一些时间尝试使用Dokku来部署它，这就是我要写的内容。

它有其缺陷，但总体而言，它在大多数情况下都有效，并且可以让我节省一些钱，所以我将它用于我的宠物项目和我的网站。

在我们开始之前，我假设你已经在你的服务器上安装了Dokku ，正确配置了所有的东西，安装了dokku-mysql ，创建了一个新的数据库（例如： ghost-db ），为 Ghost 创建了一个新的应用程序，我们简称它鬼并将它们联系起来。

从v0.24.0开始，Dokku 允许使用简单的命令部署 Docker 映像，在此之前，它是一个带有标签的噩梦。

 dokku git:from-image node-js-app dokku/node-js-getting-started:latest

所以， dokku git:from-image正是我们所需要的。

步骤 1. 部署 Ghost 映像

我们将使用可在此处找到的 Docker Ghost 映像。 您可以使用任何标签，但可以使用:latest进行初始部署。

 dokku git:from-image ghost ghost:latest

这将使用您提供的映像构建您的应用程序，然后使用您的 Dokku 设置部署它。 在该过程结束时，您将拥有一个正常工作的 Ghost 实例，没有任何持久性和错误的公共端口。

步骤 2. 处理端口

在这里，我们将通过添加一些新端口并删除我们不需要的端口来公开 Ghost 应用程序。

 dokku proxy:ports-add ghost http:80:2368 dokku proxy:ports-remove ghost http:2368:2368

在此步骤结束时，您的 Ghost 应用程序现在应该可以通过您最初提供给 Dokku 的域或通过端口映射访问。

第 3 步。坚持下去

在这一步中，我们在 Dokku 文档建议我们保留持久数据的特殊位置创建一个新文件夹ghost ，然后我们使用新文件夹/var/lib/dokku/data/storage/ghost来保留来自 docker 映像文件夹/的 Ghost 数据var/lib/ghost/内容。 这样，Ghost 创建并保存在此内容文件夹中的所有内容都将在重新加载之间保留。

步骤 4. 数据库

即使没有单独的数据库，Ghost 也可以工作，使用 SQLite 并将其保存在磁盘上，我不太喜欢它，但如果您对此没问题，请跳过此步骤。 在我们开始之前，您已经安装了 dokku-mysql 并为 Ghost 创建了一个数据库，我们将在这里需要它。

 dokku config:set \ database__client=mysql \ database__connection__database=<YOUR_DATABASE_NAME> \ database__connection__host=<YOUR_DATABASE_HOST> \ database__connection__password=<YOUR_DATABASE_PASSWORD> \ database__connection__port=<YOUR_DATABASE_PORT> \ database__connection__user=mysql

在这一步中，我们为 Ghost 应用程序提供了一些基本的数据库配置参数，当您设置它们时，Dokku 将重新启动它，然后它应该使用 mysql 数据库工作。

步骤 5. 域

我们应该做的主要事情之一就是向我们的应用程序添加一个域，Dokku 有一些有用的命令。

 dokku domains:add ghost my.ghost.website dokku config:set ghost url=http://my.ghost.website

步骤 6. SSL 证书

您的网站需要 SSL 证书。 使用 Dokku，您可以通过多种方式添加它，使用官方Let's Encypt插件，或使用您自己的证书，或使用Cloudflare SSL 。 这些都可以，但不要忽视这一步。

只是不要忘记在配置中更改您的网站网址。

 dokku config:set ghost url=https://my.ghost.website

就是这样，现在你已经有了一个由 Ghost 提供支持的工作网站，并使用 Dokku 在你自己的服务器上部署，几乎没有钱🪄🦄✨。



原文:

I started using Ghost as a headless cms for my personal website and it works like a charm, but I had to spend some time to try and deploy it using Dokku, that's what I am going to write about.

Dokku, oh, it is my favorite self-hosted deployment software for personal and sometimes commercial needs. It has its flaws but overall it works in most situations and allows me to save some money, so I use it for my pet-projects and for my website too.

Before we start, I assume you've already installed Dokku on your server, configured all the things properly, installed dokku-mysql, created a new database (ex: ghost-db), created a new app for Ghost, let's call it simply ghost and linked them.

As of v0.24.0 Dokku allows to deploy Docker images using a straightforward command, before this it was a nightmare with tagging.

dokku git:from-image node-js-app dokku/node-js-getting-started:latest

So, dokku git:from-image is exactly what we need.

Step 1. Deploy Ghost image

We are going to use Docker Ghost image that can be found here. You can use any of the tags, but it is fine to use :latest for initial deployment.

dokku git:from-image ghost ghost:latest

This will build your app using the image you provided and then deploy it using your Dokku settings. By the end of the process you will have a working Ghost instance without any persistance and wrong public ports.

Step 2. Dealing with ports

Here we are going to make Ghost app public by adding some new ports and removing those that we don't need.

dokku proxy:ports-add ghost http:80:2368
dokku proxy:ports-remove ghost http:2368:2368

By the end of this step your Ghost app should now be accessible via a domain you provided initially to Dokku or via port mapping.

Step 3. Persist it

Docker images do not keep any data between restarts, that's why we should provide some persistant volumes to it. Dokku allows us to do this easily via storage:mount command.

dokku storage:ensure-directory ghost
dokku storage:mount /var/lib/dokku/data/storage/ghost:/var/lib/ghost/content
dokku ps:rebuild ghost

In this step we create a new folder ghost in a special place where Dokku docs suggests us to keep persisted data, then we use our new folder /var/lib/dokku/data/storage/ghost to keep Ghost data from docker image folder /var/lib/ghost/content. This way everything that Ghost creates and keeps in this content folder will be persisted between reloads.

Step 4. Database

Ghost can work even without a separate database, using a SQLite and keeping it on the disk, I don't really like it but if you are okay with this, then skip this step. Before we even started, you already installed dokku-mysql and created a database for Ghost, we will need it here.

dokku config:set \
    database__client=mysql \
    database__connection__database=<YOUR_DATABASE_NAME> \
    database__connection__host=<YOUR_DATABASE_HOST> \
    database__connection__password=<YOUR_DATABASE_PASSWORD> \
    database__connection__port=<YOUR_DATABASE_PORT> \
    database__connection__user=mysql

In this step we provide some essential database configuration params to the Ghost app, as you set them, Dokku will restart it and it should then work using mysql database.

Step 5. Domain

One of the main things we should do, is to add a domain to our app, Dokku has some useful commands for this.

dokku domains:add ghost my.ghost.website
dokku config:set ghost url=http://my.ghost.website

Step 6. SSL certificate

You need SSL certificate for your website. With Dokku you can add it in several ways, using official Let's Encypt plugin, or using your own certificate, or using a Cloudflare SSL. Any of these do, but don't neglect this step.

Just don't forget to change your website url in configs.

dokku config:set ghost url=https://my.ghost.website

Thats it it, by now you have a working website powered by Ghost and deployed using Dokku on your own server for little to no money 🪄🦄✨.
item.author: bitkidd

item.title: 为什么你的 API 应该明确地将资源所有者 ID 作为参数而不是从身份验证中推断。 | Why your API should explicitly take resource owner id as a parameter instead of inferring from authentication.
item.description:
想象一下，您正在设计一个 REST API 来处理资源（例如订单）。 假设我们使用 JWT 令牌进行身份验证。 令牌在声明有效负载中具有userId 。

 { "sub" : "1234567890" , "name" : "John Doe" , "iat" : 1516239022 , "userId" : "..."
}

经典方法

然后，您可以选择从令牌中推断出userId并使用它来检查授权。

 @GetMapping ( "/orders/{orderId}" ) public Order getOrderDetail ( String orderId ) { String userId = getUserIdFromAuthMiddlewareSomehow (); Order order = getOrderFromDatabase ( orderId ); if (! order . getUserId (). equals ( userId )) { throw new RuntimeException ( "Not authorized" ); } return order ; }

如果您选择此方法，您的 API 调用可能如下所示。

 jwt_token = ... curl -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ jwt_token } " https://your-api.example.com/orders/12345

这种方法有两个缺点。

  1. 即使调用者用户未经授权，您的 API 也需要运行一些（如果不是完整的话）业务逻辑并访问数据库。 如果此 API 消耗大量服务器的资源和性能，这将更加严重。

  2. 您可以在业务逻辑中混合身份验证问题，以便将其分开。 想象一下，您的 API 有一个复杂的实现，并且您不仅希望在调用者是资源所有者本身的情况下重用它，而且还希望对管理员用户重用它，等等。 检查“谁”的复杂逻辑将分散在各处，最终将更难理解您的 API 打算做什么。

更好的方法

另一种选择是将userId显式放入 API 参数中，并将授权逻辑移至 AOP 中间件。 例如，您可以编写一个自定义注解@CheckTokenPayload来告诉中间件要检查哪个字段。

 @CheckTokenPayload ( userId = "#userId" ) @GetMapping ( "/get-order" ) public Order getOrderDetail ( @Param String orderId , @Param String userId ) { const order = getOrderFromDatabase ( orderId , userId ); return order ; }

使用这种方法，你的业务逻辑是干净的，如果用户没有被授权，你的 API 不需要执行任何资源消耗逻辑。

但是在从数据库中查询时，您必须小心将userId与orderId一起使用，否则如果经过身份验证的用户知道orderId ，他将可以访问该订单。

多维所有权

在现实世界中，它不会像检查资源所有者的userId那样简单，因为资源可以有多种“所有者”。

例如，商户也可以是订单的所有者，这使得商户的部分员工有资格查看订单。

并且，将有管理人员、支持（呼叫中心）人员等。

幸运的是，系统的用户类型是非动态的方面。 您始终可以设计访问令牌和中间件来处理每种类型的用户。 例如，您的 JWT 令牌可能看起来像。

 // token payload for customer user
{ "sub" : "1234567890" , "name" : "John Doe" , "iat" : 1516239022 , "userType" : "CUSTOMER" , "customerId" : "..."
} // token payload for merchant staff
{ "sub" : "1234567890" , "name" : "John Doe" , "iat" : 1516239022 , "userType" : "MERCHANT_STAFF" , "merchantStaffId" : "..." , "merchantId" : "..." , "permissions" : [ "order:view" , "order:edit" , ... ]
}

这是一个编写 API 的示例，它可以检查并允许不同类型的用户，而无需接触数据库。

 @CheckTokenPayload ( authTypeAllows = { "CUSTOMER" }, customerId = "#customerId" ) @GetMapping ( "/customer/get-order" ) public Order getOrderDetailForCustomer ( @Param String orderId , @Param String customerId ) { const order = getOrderFromDatabase ( orderId , userId ); return order ; } @CheckTokenPayload ( authType = { "MERCHANT_STAFF" , "ADMIN_STAFF" }, // you may write the middleware to omit merchantId check if the authType is ADMIN_STAFF merchantId = "#merchantId" , permissionsContain = { "order:view" } ) @GetMapping ( "/merchant/get-order" ) public Order getOrderDetailForMerchantOrAdmin ( @Param String orderId , @Param String merchantId ) { const order = getOrderFromDatabase ( orderId , merchantId ); return order ; }


原文:

Imagine your are designing a REST API to process a resource (e.g. an order). The API should be restricted to be available only when the caller user is authenticated and he is the owner of the resource.

Suppose that we are using JWT token for authentication. The token has the userId in the claim payload.

{ "sub": "1234567890", "name": "John Doe", "iat": 1516239022, "userId": "..."
}

The classic approach

You then have an option to infer the userId from the token and use it to check for the authorization.

@GetMapping("/orders/{orderId}")
public Order getOrderDetail(String orderId) { String userId = getUserIdFromAuthMiddlewareSomehow(); Order order = getOrderFromDatabase(orderId); if (!order.getUserId().equals(userId)) { throw new RuntimeException("Not authorized"); } return order;
}

If you choose this approach, your API call might look like this.

jwt_token=...
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer ${jwt_token}" https://your-api.example.com/orders/12345

This approach has 2 drawbacks.

  1. Your API will need to run some, if not full, business logic and access the database even though the caller user is not authorized. This will be more serious if this API consumes great server's resource and performance.

  2. You mix the authentication concern in your business logic where it can be separated. Imagine your API has a complex implementation, and you want to reuse it for not only when the caller is the resource owner itself, but also for an administrator user as well, and so on. The complex logic to check for the "who" will be scattered all over the place, eventually it will be harder to understand "what" your API intends to do.

The better approach

Another option is to put the userId explicitly in the API parameter, and move the authorization logic to AOP middleware. For example, you can write a custom annotation @CheckTokenPayload to tell the middleware which field to check.

@CheckTokenPayload( userId = "#userId"
)
@GetMapping("/get-order")
public Order getOrderDetail(@Param String orderId, @Param String userId) { const order = getOrderFromDatabase(orderId, userId); return order;
}

With this approach, your business logic is clean, and your API does not need to execute any resource consumption logic if the user is not authorized.

But you must be careful to use the userId together with the orderId when query from the database, otherwise the order will be accessible to authenticated user if he know the orderId.

Multi-dimensional ownership

In real world, it will not be as simple as checking the resource owner's userId because a resource can has many kinds of "owner".

For example, a merchant can also be an owner of an order, which makes some of the merchant's staffs eligible to see the order.

And, there will administrator staffs, support (call center) staffs, etc.

Fortunately, the user types of the system is the aspect that is not dynamic. You can always design your access token and the middleware to handle every type of the user. For example, your JWT token might look like.

// token payload for customer user
{ "sub": "1234567890", "name": "John Doe", "iat": 1516239022, "userType": "CUSTOMER", "customerId": "..."
} // token payload for merchant staff
{ "sub": "1234567890", "name": "John Doe", "iat": 1516239022, "userType": "MERCHANT_STAFF", "merchantStaffId": "...", "merchantId": "...", "permissions": ["order:view", "order:edit", ...]
}

And this is an example write APIs that checks and allows different types of the user without having to touch the database.

@CheckTokenPayload( authTypeAllows = {"CUSTOMER"}, customerId = "#customerId"
)
@GetMapping("/customer/get-order")
public Order getOrderDetailForCustomer(@Param String orderId, @Param String customerId) { const order = getOrderFromDatabase(orderId, userId); return order;
} @CheckTokenPayload( authType = {"MERCHANT_STAFF", "ADMIN_STAFF"}, // you may write the middleware to omit merchantId check if the authType is ADMIN_STAFF merchantId = "#merchantId", permissionsContain = {"order:view"}
)
@GetMapping("/merchant/get-order")
public Order getOrderDetailForMerchantOrAdmin(@Param String orderId, @Param String merchantId) { const order = getOrderFromDatabase(orderId, merchantId); return order;
}

item.author: abdessalambou

item.title: 为什么手动测试将在行业中盛行 | Why Manual Testing Is Going To Prevail The Industry
item.description:
不幸的是，质量分析师经常赢得坏人的名声。 当有人告诉他们，他们的代码错误并且应用程序的主要部分因此而崩溃时，开发人员通常会感到沮丧。 但事实是，测试人员和开发人员一样关心软件。 他们两个一起工作的最终结果是一个令人愉快的无错误应用程序。

在自动化测试的新时代，手动测试被认为是不必要的和人力的浪费。 在看到一个自动化程序快速执行整个测试用例库后，对自动化测试的信任和信心会大大增加。 但是，手动测试的价值永远不可低估，并且有一些原因导致手动测试仍然盛行。

这个针对初学者的手动测试教程，将帮助您在 LambdaTest 平台上进行实时交互式实时测试。

另外，查看css 媒体分辨率- css-media-resolution 是一个 CSS3 模块，它允许使用最小/最大分辨率的替代方法创建媒体查询。

浏览器兼容性测试错误

我并不是说浏览器兼容性测试不能自动化。 然而，手动探索性测试对于发现浏览器兼容性问题，尤其是视觉布局问题同样重要。

即使是自动化也会出错

自动化只不过是手动输入的脚本。 就像填充错误的代码一样，它可能会失败。 错误的自动化脚本可以推断为您的应用程序中的功能失败，最坏的情况是作为正确功能传递的代码中的错误。 当手动测试代码时，可以确保从用户的角度测试应用程序，没有出错的余地。

你知道CSS Mixblendmode -CSS mix-blend-mode 属性是一项实验性功能，允许在 HTML 元素和自定义 SVG 元素之间进行混合。

自动化测试只是另一种工具

自动化测试不仅仅是结束手动测试，更多的是节省执行重复工作所浪费的时间，以便手动测试人员可以提出创新的测试用例场景。 充分利用自动化测试的最佳方式是创建独特的脚本并增强应用程序的整体覆盖率。

小型项目的自动化成本高昂

在选择自动化测试时，组织必须牢记以下几点

  • 自动化软件本身成本高

  • 软件的管理和维护不是免费的

  • 由于软件设置和处理造成的时间损失

  • 敏捷中的脚本编写和重写可能很耗时

  • 在大型应用程序的情况下，所消耗的成本和时间是可以承受的。 但是对于较小的项目，自动化测试可能会浪费大量金钱和时间。 手动测试仪可以以低得多的成本完成需要完成的工作。

用户体验验证

在应用程序的用户界面中使用自动化测试来响应响应受到广泛欢迎。 但是，它不应该是一种帮助，而是增强您的 UI 测试能力。 自动化脚本中的一个小错误将导致对此类对齐方式的误解。 手动测试人员可以立即检测到 UI 中是否有些“不正常”，并将测试用例标记为“失败”。

总是有例外

有些场景无法自动化。 由于测试场景的复杂性和技术的限制，与手动执行相比，自动化场景的成本太高。 假设客户需要测试其移动钱包应用程序的触碰付款功能。 当您可以简单地使用移动设备手动测试时，开发一种自动化场景的方法既耗时又不可能。

测试技能基础

只有自动化的最终结果是富有成效的。 脚本的开发和框架的设置并不容易。 一个有效的自动化测试人员除了编程技能基础外，还应该具备理解测试用例的知识和专业知识。 一个人可以通过多年的手动质量分析经验获得这些技能。 在短时间内获得具有这些特定技能的测试人员几乎是不可能的。 只有经过适当培训的高效手动测试人员才能完成这项工作。

新领域探索

移动优先趋势开始主导市场的时间并不长。 智能手机正在定义应用程序的外观。 目前，手机已经普及，一种新型硬件正在兴起。 可穿戴设备，被预测为工作场所生产力和沟通的下一个最佳选择。

在测试方面，可穿戴设备领域几乎是未开发的。 开发能够有效探索设备是否可用或是否存在任何错误的自动化测试用例场景将很困难。 从长远来看，手动测试的成本会更低，并且将是在此硬件上进行测试的最佳方法。

你知道CSS 命名空间吗？ 这允许将当前命名空间以外的命名空间中的元素作为目标，从而允许复合和复杂的选择器。

快速重现生产中的错误。

尽管有希望，但在将应用程序部署到生产环境之前，并非所有错误都得到修复。 客户通常会在检测到错误时向支持团队提出票证。 对于所有基于 Web 的应用程序，修补程序是必须的。 手动测试人员可以查看最终用户提交的信息，快速重现错误并告知开发人员错误实际发生的位置。 通过自动化测试，可以延迟修复和客户问题之间的时间。

引用

作为测试人员，我们最重要的目标通常是了解威胁我们产品价值的问题，以便我们的客户能够有效地处理这些问题。 我们的测试客户和使用软件的人都没有将他们遇到的问题分为“手动错误”和“自动错误”。 因此，让我们认识和欣赏技术测试人员、测试工具工匠和我们工艺中的其他专家。 让我们不要将他们视为“手动测试人员”。 让我们结束“手动测试”。
 尽管组织正在选择敏捷环境，但不会完全放弃熟悉的流程。 尽管人工测试可能不是任何地方的最佳选择，但在任何应用程序的测试阶段总会出现一些场景，届时人工评估最终将成为保持产品质量的唯一方法。 通过清楚地了解手动测试人员的能力，QA 团队可以使用它来提高自动化测试的优势。



原文:

A quality analyst unfortunately often earns the reputation of being a bad guy. It often becomes frustrating for a developer when someone tells them, their code is wrong and a major part of an application crashed because of that. But the truth is, testers care about the software same as developers. The end result of both of them working together is an enjoyable bug free application.

In the new age of automation testing, manual testing is being framed as unnecessary and a waste of manpower. After watching an automation program execute an entire test case library swiftly, trust and faith in automation testing can highly increase. However, the value of manual testing can never be underestimated, and there are some reasons for which manual testing is still prevailing.

This manual testing tutorial for beginners, will help you perform live interactive Real-Time Testing on LambdaTest platform.

Also, check out css media resolution- css-media-resolution is a CSS3 module that allows the creation of media queries using an alternative method to min/max-resolution.

Browser Compatibility Testing Errors

One of the main driving force behind LambdaTest cross browser compatibility testing platform was to empower testers by giving them tools to fast track browser testing and exploratory testing process. I am not saying that browser compatibility testing cannot be automated. However manual exploratory testing is equally important in finding browser compatibility issues, especially visual layout issues.

Even Automation Can Have Error

Automation is nothing but a manually entered script. Just like an error filled code it can fail. An incorrect automation script can be deduced as a functionality that failed in your application, worst case scenario being an error in the code being passed as a correct functionality. When a code is manually tested, it is ensured that the application is being tested from a user’s point of view with no room for errors.

Did you know CSS Mixblendmode-The CSS mix-blend-mode property is an experimental feature that allows for blending between HTML elements and custom SVG elements.

Automation Testing is Just Another Tool

Automation testing is not all about putting an end to manual testing, its more about saving the time wasted in performing repetitive jobs, so that manual testers can come up with innovative test case scenarios. The best way automation testing can be utilized fully Is by creating unique scripts and enhancing overall coverage of an application.

Automation is Costly for Small Projects

When opting for automation testing, the organization has to keep the following things in mind

  • The automation software itself is costly

  • Management and maintenance of the software is not free

  • Loss of time because of software setup and processing

  • Script writing and rewriting in Agile can be time consuming

  • The cost and time consumed can be afforded in the case of a large application. But in case of smaller projects, automation testing can prove to be a considerable waste of money and time. A manual tester can do the job needed to be done at a much lower cost.

Validation of User Experience

The use of automated testing for responsiveness in the user interface of an application is widely appreciated. However, it is not supposed to be an aid, but a boost to your UI testing capabilities. Automation testing in UI works by checking the placement of an element, alignment and distance between elements and the container. A small error in the automation script will result in misinterpretation of one such alignment. A manual tester can immediately detect if something looks a little “off” in the UI and mark the test case as “fail”.

There Are Always Exceptions

Some scenarios cannot be automated. Because of complexity of the test scenario and limitations of technology, cost of automating the scenario is too high when compared to manual execution. Let’s suppose a customer needs to test the tap and pay functionality for his mobile wallet application. Developing a way for automating the scenario is time consuming and impossible when you can simply test it manually with your mobile device.

Foundation of Testing Skills

Only the end result of automation is fruitful. The development of scripts and framework set up is not at all easy. An effective automation tester should have the knowledge and expertise of understanding test cases apart from foundation of programming skills. A person can acquire those skillsets over years of experience in manual quality analysis. Acquiring a tester with those specific skill sets is virtually impossible at a short notice. Only an efficient manual tester can do the job when trained properly.

New Domains to Explore

It hasn’t been that long when mobile first trend started dominating the market. Smartphones were defining what an application is supposed to look like. At present, phones have become common and a new kind of hardware is emerging. Wearables, being predicted as the next best thing for productivity and communication in the workplace.

When it comes to testing, the field of wearables is almost unexplored. It will be difficult to develop automated test case scenarios that will efficiently explore whether the device is workable or if there is any bug. In the long run, manual testing will cost less and will be the best approach to testing on this hardware.

Do you know about CSS namespaces? The @namespace at-rule can be used to create pipes (|) in selectors. This allows for elements from namespaces other than the current one to be targeted, allowing for compound and complex selectors.

Fast Reproduction of Errors in Production.

In spite of hopes, not all errors are fixed before an application is deployed into production. Customers usually raises ticket to the support team whenever they detect an error. For all web-based applications, hotfixes are a must. A manual tester can go through the information submitted by end users, quickly reproduce the error and inform the developer about where the error is actually happening. With automation testing, the time between a fix and a customer issue is delayed.

QUOTE

Our most important goal as testers, typically, is to learn about problems that threaten value in our products, so that our clients can deal with those problems effectively. Neither our testing clients nor people who use software divide the problems they experience into “manual bugs” and “automated bugs”. So let’s recognize and admire technical testers, testing toolsmiths and the other specialists in our craft. Let us not dismiss them as “manual testers”. Let’s put an end to “manual testing”.
-Micheal Bolton — http://www.developsense.com/blog/2017/11/the-end-of-manual-testing/

Although organizations are opting for agile environments, familiar processes will not be abandoned completely. Although manual testing may not be the best choice everywhere, some scenarios will always arise in any application’s testing phase when human evaluation will eventually become the only way to maintain the product’s quality. By getting a clear idea of a manual tester’s capability, QA teams can use it to boost the benefits of automation testing.
item.author: arnabroychowdhury

item.title: 为什么最佳 MVP 开发公司对您的业务很重要？ | Why is Best MVP Development Company Important for Your Business?
item.description:
所以你对产品有想法吗？ 伟大的！ 现在，您想在此基础上构建一个应用程序，太棒了！ 您是否正在考虑获得病毒并赚取巨额利润？ 很公平。

可是等等！

你做过市场分析或检查你的同行竞争对手吗？ 您是否知道您的产品是否可行、可接受且可扩展以渗透市场并成为领导者？

这就是 MVP（最小可行产品）方法帮助许多初创公司取得成功的地方。 您可以通过构建 MVP 来使用真实客户验证强制性功能，从而轻松扩展您的产品。

本博客将帮助您了解 MVP 开发公司使用的方法，并为您提供一些优势来发展您的业务。 我们来看一下。

分析市场需求
大多数新企业失败是因为他们的想法没有市场需求。 他们在没有分析其市场需求的情况下，以创造性的眼光瞄准过高。 它可以防止任何企业在不知道用户是否会珍惜它的情况下将其所有努力、精力、资产等投入到一个项目上。

吸引早期采用者
使用最小可行方法的另一个优点是它有助于吸引早期采用者。 这些可能是您的同伴、亲戚、队友或您的应用程序的其他早期用户。

主要好处是您不必创建最终产品来在这些早期采用者身上进行测试。 您所需要的只是产品的测试版，您可以将其传授给早期用户。

测试用户体验功能
平面设计师的角色在任何 MVP 开发公司中都是至关重要的。 目前的用户具有数字意识，只要求最好的用户体验。 在卸载应用程序之前，他们甚至不会考虑是否发现任何可怕的用户体验迹象。

任何新产品开发的业务目标不仅仅是下载您的应用程序，还包括留住您的用户。 您可以使用最低限度的可行产品在真正的用户身上测试您的产品。 它将让您知道用户在早期可能遇到的问题。

确认货币化战略
拥有防失败的货币化策略对于从任何产品中获得令人难以置信的利润至关重要。 今天，市场上有许多货币化策略。 您可以从完全付费的服务、应用内购买、促销和众多选择中进行选择。

通过在您的业务中实施 MVP 方法，您可以测试哪种策略最适合货币化。 您可以收集用户的意见，并检查他们是否准备为您通过应用程序获利的功能付费。

快速发布
选择仅创建具有核心功能的产品会显着加快发布速度。 发布产品的第一个版本后，您可以立即测试市场情绪并收集用户对主要功能和所需功能的评论。

会有人在你之前发布一个类似的好应用程序。 做出勇敢的努力，尽量减少最初的发布，然后一步一步地向前迈进。 它会让你更容易接受市场。

最后的想法
为了在新产品进入市场时节省您宝贵的时间、费用和资源，我们建议考虑 MVP。 这样的公司可以帮助您进行 MVP 开发并扩展您的业务。 我希望这个 MVP 开发指南可以帮助您了解细节并在发布产品之前做出最佳决策。



原文:

So you have an idea about a product? Great! Now, you want to build an app on that, superb! Are you thinking of getting viral and earning huge profits? Fair enough.

But, wait!

Have you done the market analysis or checked your peer competitors? Do you know if your product is viable, acceptable, and scalable to penetrate the market and be a leader?

That is where the MVP(Minimum Viable Product) approach has helped many startups succeed. You can effortlessly scale your product by verifying the mandatory features using real customers by building an MVP.

This blog will help you know the approach that an MVP development company uses and provide you with several advantages to grow your business. Let's have a look.

Analyze Market Demand
Most new businesses fail because their idea has no market requirement. They aim excessively high with a creative vision without analyzing its market need. An MVP is a prototype of the primary product that assists you with giving a shape to your thought.

MVP development organization adopts the iterative strategy, which permits you to test the idea without investing total funds. It keeps any business from investing all of its endeavors, energy, assets, and so on in one item without knowing whether users will cherish it or not.

Attracting Early Adopters
One more advantage of utilizing a minimum viable approach is that it helps in attracting early adopters. These could be your companions, relatives, teammates, or other early users of your application.

The primary benefit is that you do not have to create the final product to test it on these early adopters. All you require is a beta version of the product, and you can impart it to the early users.

Test UX Functionality
The part of a graphic designer is pivotal in any MVP development firm. The present users are digitally aware and request only the best user experience. Before uninstalling an application, they do not even think if they find even the slightest hint of a horrendous user experience.

The business objective of any new product development is not simply to download your app but also to retain your users. You can test your product on genuine users with a minimum viable product. It will let you know the problems that users may encounter early on.

Confirming Monetization Strategy
To have a fail-proof monetization strategy is crucial to enjoying incredible profits from any product. Today, there are many monetization strategies present in the market. One can select from totally paid services, in-app purchases, promotions, and numerous choices.

By implementing the MVP approach in your business, you can test which strategy is fantastic for monetization. You can gather inputs from your users and check whether they are prepared to pay for features in which you have monetized your application.

Fast Release
The choice to create the product only with core functions remarkably accelerates the release. After releasing the first version of your product, you can test market sentiments instantly and gather users' reviews on both primary and desired features.

There will be somebody who can release a similar good application before you. Put forth a valiant effort to keep your initial release minimal, and then move ahead step by step. It will make you considerably more receptive to the market.

Final Thoughts
To save your valuable time, expenses, and resources while entering the market with a new product, we suggest considering an MVP. Such a company can help you with the MVP development and scale your business. I hope this MVP development guide helps you know the ins and outs and make the best decision before launching your product.
item.author: alionamill

item.title: 对作为软件工程师的技术职业感到失望 | Becoming disillusioned with career in tech as a software engineer
item.description:
大约 6 年前（几乎到今天），我从 SWE 学位毕业。 我去的大学不是你认识的那些花哨的大牌之一，而是英国威尔士的一个相当小的大学。 它的课程相当不错，让我具备了在伦敦找到工作的必要技能。 不过最近，我开始质疑作为一名软件工程师对我个人来说意味着什么。

循环
在过去的 6 年里，我一直在多家公司工作。 在每一个中，我都觉得有些东西还不够。 鉴于我所在公司的限制，我将达到不再学习的地步，寻找一份拥有更新技术和更高薪水的新工作，冲洗并重复。 我觉得我已经陷入这个循环有一段时间了。 我已经达到了对一些最复杂的系统类型有很好理解的地步。 我曾与单体、微服务、各种不同的数据库和消息代理一起工作，并且已经达到了非领导/管理角色的薪水，这在我所居住的城市中处于最高百分位。


也许，我一直在通过我所做的工作寻找意义的感觉。 曾经有过这样的感觉，但随着时间的推移，我感觉在那些时刻我一直在对自己撒谎。 这段时间总是结束，我一遍又一遍地感到毫无意义。 这是为什么？

弄清楚事情

我对自己所做的事情感到惊奇的时刻总是我会尝试创造一些新的东西——连接到一个有趣的新 API，一个对我来说听起来很有趣的想法的概念证明，或者（最近）制作音乐。 在我看来，它们之间的共同点始终是创造不存在的东西的创造性方面。 从字面上看，我已经能够创造出不存在的东西。 这有多神奇？ 这就是我喜欢软件工程的地方——你从无到有创造东西！ 有人可能会争辩说，即使是一份普通的工作，这正是你所得到的，但归根结底，除非你处于一个非常早期的初创公司，否则会有人给你一份你只需要实施的需求清单. 当然这并没有错，但无论出于何种原因，我不再觉得它令人兴奋……有一次，我痴迷于进入一家 FAANG 公司的想法。 这种渴望似乎也消退了。

然而，我一直被那种感觉像是艺术的创作行为所吸引。 我认为所有人本质上都是有创造力的生物。 正如 Yuval Harari 在他的《智人》一书中所描述的，想象力是人类进步的关键驱动力之一。 我们可以从字面上想象明天或后天的样子，并将其变为现实。 这就是创造力和艺术的工作方式！ 许多人谈论潜在的失业，因为世界上越来越多的事情被机器人和人工智能自动化，但我认为人类有足够的创造力，总是想办法打发时间并以此谋生……

下一步是什么？

所以现在，在我意识到我害怕每天为某人工作的朝九晚五的无休止的 while 循环之后，我终于可以明确地说我想要追求既具有创造性又能产生收入的东西。 我还没有信心打造像亚马逊或特斯拉这样改变游戏规则的产品，就像所有硅谷人所追求的那样。 不过，我认为还有另一种方法——我很乐意从事的小型独立产品组合现在似乎是一个不错的选择。 我确信要做到这一点并不容易，而且我不会很快辞职。 目前我计划尝试构建辅助项目并发展它们。 你有过类似的想法和经历吗？ 或者也许你已经经历过这种转变？ 我很乐意在这里在 Twitter 上联系并谈论它！



原文:

I graduated from my SWE degree around 6 years ago (almost to the date). The uni I went to wasn’t one of those fancy big names you’d recognise but a rather small one in Wales, UK. It had a fairly decent curriculum and gave me the necessary skills to get a job in London. Lately though, I’ve started questioning what it means for me personally to work as a software engineer.

The loop
For the past 6 years I’ve been working my way through various companies. In each one I’ve felt like something just isn’t enough. I would reach a point where I’m not learning anymore given the constraints of the company I’m in, look for a new job with newer tech and a bigger salary, rinse and repeat. I feel I’ve been stuck in this loop for a while now. I’ve reached a point where I have a good understanding of some of the most complex types of systems. I’ve worked with monoliths, microservices, various different databases and message brokers and have reached a salary which for a non-lead/management role would be somewhere in the top percentiles for the city I live in.


Perhaps, I’ve been searching for the feeling of meaning through the work I do. There have been times I’ve felt this but as time goes on it feels like I’ve been lying to myself in those moments. ‘If I work for a company that gives credit to small businesses, this directly benefits the economy and has a net positive impact of the wider society I live in’ is what I would tell myself and it would give me this sense of pleasure for a while. This period always ends though and I feel meaningless over and over again. Why is that?

Figuring things out

The moments that I’ve felt amazing about what I do were always the ones where I would try to create something new - hook up to an interesting new API, a proof of concept for an idea that sounds fun to me or (more recently) making music. It seems to me that the common denominator between these was always the creative aspect of building something which doesn’t exist. Literally something which has not existed that I’ve been able to create. How amazing is that? This is what I love about software engineering - you create something from nothing! One might argue that that’s exactly what you get even with a regular job, but at the end of the day, unless you’re in a really early stage startup, there will be someone giving you a list of requirements that you just need to implement. Of course there’s nothing wrong with that but for whatever reason, I no longer find it exciting… At one point I was obsessed with the idea of getting into one of the FAANG companies. That desire seems to have faded as well.

However, I’ve always been drawn to that act of creation which feels like art. I think all humans are intrinsically creative creatures. As Yuval Harari describes in his book Sapiens, imagination is one of the key drivers of humanity’s progress. We can literally conjure an image of what tomorrow or the day after will look like and make this a reality. This is how creativity and art work! Many people talk about potential joblessness as more things in the world are automated by robots and AI, but I think humans are creative enough to always think of ways to spend their time and make a living out of that...

What's next?

So now, after I’ve realized that I dread this endless while loop of the 9 to 6 grind every day working for someone, I finally have the clarity to say I want to pursue something that is both creative and can generate revenue. I don’t yet have the confidence to build a game-changing product like Amazon or Tesla like all the Silicon Valley dudes aim for. I think there’s an alternative way though - a portfolio of smaller indie products that I’d be happy to work on seems like a good alternative right now. I’m sure it’s not easy to achieve that and I won’t be quitting my job soon. For the time being I plan to try build side projects and grow them. Have you ever had similar thoughts and experience? Or perhaps you’ve already gone through this type of transition? I’d be happy to connect on Twitter here and chat about that!
item.author: martinzokov

item.title: 从建造到销售 - EP.13 | From building to selling - EP.13
item.description:
欢迎来到 Crossroad 构建之旅的第 13 天！ 本系列讲述了我从构建到销售的独立黑客产品之旅。 如果你错过了第十二天，你可以在这里查看。 如果您想观看重播，我还将今天的Twitch 流上传到YouTube 。

第一个 Crossroad 网站上线了！ 今天，我的主要关注点是构建第一个使用 crossroad 作为 UI 的站点。 有什么比在生物页面中创建我自己的链接更好的项目呢？ 毕竟，如果作者不使用它，你为什么要使用它？

我决定使用 NextJS 在生物站点中建立我的链接。 我总是可以使用这个框架快速启动一个站点，并且它还支持足够的开箱即用，用于我在路上需要的所有数据获取。

我决定在生物页面中为我的这个版本的链接硬编码所有内容。 虽然我计划从我的数据库中获取我的特色博客文章，但我今天的主要关注点是获取任何剩余的错误和缺失的功能。

事实证明，特别是在 Safari（如果你问我的话，是新的 IE）上，有很多令人讨厌的（但很容易修复）错误。 例如，由于另一个 flexbox 内部的 flexbox，我的整个社交行不可见。 不到两个小时后，我记下了很多东西并改进了模板的某些方面，我在简历页面中的链接终于上线了！ 是的，你读得很好，LIVE！

如果您想查看该网站的实际运行情况，请点击以下链接：

为发布周做准备。 现在我有了产品的最终“待修复”列表，我终于有了发布日期。 我将在以下时间推出产品：

所以我有大约三天的时间来完成模板，把它放在 Gumroad 上，为登陆页面购买一个域名，并准备发布，包括副本和资产。 这似乎是可行的，所以让我们开始吧！****

下（发布）周见！ 随着links.twankrui.com的上线，我们已经确定了在上线之前要克服的最后障碍。 总而言之，在我们下周四上线之前，我还需要两天的修复和准备！

我对下周的发布感到非常兴奋。 我希望你能分享一些这样的兴奋。

再次感谢您阅读此构建日志。 祝你有美好的一天，我们下周二见！

如果您想同时保持更新，请在 Twitter 上关注我。

有爱，

旺克鲁伊



原文:

Welcome to day thirteen of the Crossroad build journey! This series follows my journey of indie hacking a product from building to selling. If you missed day twelve, you could check it out here. I also uploaded today's Twitch stream to YouTube if you want to watch the replay.

The first Crossroad site is live! 🚀

Today my primary focus was building the first site that uses crossroad for its UI. What better project to take on than creating my own link in bio page? After all, if the author doesn't use it, why should you?

I decided to build my link in bio site with NextJS. I can always spin a site up quickly using this framework, and it also supports enough out of the box for all the data fetching I'll need down the road.

I decided to hardcode everything for this version of my link in bio page. While I plan on fetching my featured blog posts from my database, my main focus today was fetching any remaining bugs and missing features.

It turned out, especially on Safari (the new IE, if you ask me), that there were quite a few nasty (but easy to fix) bugs. For example, my entire social row wasn't visible because of a flexbox inside of another flexbox. 🤦‍♂️

After a little under two hours, with a lot of jotting things down and improving certain aspects of the template, my link in bio page was finally live! Yeah, you read that well, LIVE!

If you want to see the site in action, here is the link:

links.twankrui.com

Prepping for launch week. 🚚

Now that I have the product's final "to-fix" list, I finally have a launch date. I'll launch the product on:

Thursday, June 9

So I have roughly three days to wrap up the template, put it up on Gumroad, buy a domain for the landing page, and prepare the launch, including the copy and assets. It seems doable, so let's go for it!****

See you next (launch) week! 👋

With links.twankrui.com up in the air, we have defined the final roadblocks to overcome before going live. All in all, I need two more days of fixing and preparation before we can go live next Thursday!

I am incredibly excited for the launch next week. I hope you share some of this excitement.

Thanks again for reading this build log. Have a great day, and I’ll see you next Tuesday!

If you want to stay updated in the meantime, give me a follow on Twitter.

With love,

Twankrui
item.author: twankrui

item.title: 身临其境的电子学习——物有所值 | Immersive eLearning — The Right Bang for your Buck
item.description:
选择引人入胜的沉浸式学习体验可以为您的企业在线学习提供具有成本效益的优势。
每次我们遇到正在寻找其他培训方式的客户时，他们通常会首先观察到他们现有的培训方法——讲师指导培训 (ILT) 或“粉笔和谈话”——并不理想。 他们说组织起来很乏味，学习者以后没有机会跟进/澄清，并且保留不是最佳的。 但 ILT 最紧迫的问题是投资回报率微薄。 事实上，一位客户曾经说过，“我们常常觉得我们所做的投资没有得到任何有形的东西。 学习化为乌有，我们常常没有什么可回头的”。

那么，为什么要考虑身临其境的电子学习呢？
让我们从底线开始：执行成本要低得多； 几乎可以自由扩展。 这样想，一旦准备好，沉浸式电子学习的复制成本是最低的； 场地、后勤、培训师等无额外费用。

学习永无止境：通过沉浸式电子学习，您的员工可以获得积极主动的学习解决方案，这些解决方案很好地集成并在逻辑上映射到学习者对概念的理解。 他们可以在不增加生产成本的情况下学习、修改和重新学习——您的员工可以根据需要进行多次学习。

盲区无损失：借助沉浸式电子学习，您可以衡量员工的学习情况。 良好的衡量使您能够根据员工最适合的工作重新技能、提升技能、分配和重新分配您的员工。

不因不一致而损失学习：沉浸式电子学习提供了培训交付的一致性（接近于精确性）——即使您每次都让同一位培训师进行 ILT，您也无法确定一致性，对吗？

无需浪费时间等待：沉浸式电子学习提供廉价、方便的扩展。 无需担心满足 ROI 的最小班级规模； 在等待中不会损失生产时间。 您的员工可以立即接受培训和部署。

每次我们遇到正在寻找其他培训方式的客户时，他们通常会首先观察到他们现有的培训方法——讲师指导培训 (ILT) 或“粉笔和谈话”——并不理想。 他们说组织起来很乏味，学习者以后没有机会跟进/澄清，并且保留不是最佳的。 但 ILT 最紧迫的问题是投资回报率微薄。 事实上，一位客户曾经说过，“我们常常觉得我们所做的投资没有得到任何有形的东西。 学习化为乌有，我们常常没有什么可回头的”。

原文:

Choosing engaging and immersive learning experiences can give your corporate eLearning the cost-effective edge.
Every time we meet clients who are looking for alternate ways to deliver their training, they often start with the observation that their existing training method — instructor-led training (ILT) or ‘Chalk and Talk’ — isn’t ideal. They say that it’s tedious to organize, learners don’t have the opportunity for follow-ups /clarification later, and that retention is not optimal. But the most pressing concern with ILT is the meagre return on investment. In fact, a client once said, “Often we feel like we don’t get anything tangible for the investment we make. The learning dissolves into thin air, and we are often left with nothing to go back to”.

So, why should you consider immersive eLearning?
Let’s start with the bottom line: It is significantly less expensive to execute; almost free to scale. Think of it this way, once ready, the replication cost of immersive eLearning is minimal; no additional expense on the venue, logistics, trainer, etc.

No end to learning: With immersive eLearning, your employees get proactive learning solutions that are well integrated and logically mapped to the learner's understanding of the concepts. They can learn, revise and re-learn at no additional production cost — your employees can learn as many times as they need.

No loss on the blind side: With immersive eLearning, you can measure your employees’ learning. good measurement enables you to re-skill, up-skill, assign, and reassign your employees based on what they are best suited for.

No loss of learning due to inconsistency: Immersive eLearning offers consistency (bordering on exactitude) of training delivery — even if you get the same trainer to do the ILT every time, you can’t be sure of consistency, can you?

No loss of time in waiting: Immersive eLearning offers inexpensive, convenient scaling. There is no need to worry about the minimum class size to meet ROI; no loss of productive time in waiting. Your employees can be instantly trained and deployed.

When we say cost-efficient, we don’t mean that immersive learning solution is cheap. It does come at a price. Hornbill FX is one of the best 3d immersive learning solutions companies in India. We offer turnkey immersive eLearning solutions, using a combination of 3D animation, 2D animation, Augmented Reality, Virtual Reality, and well-honed principles of instructional design to create truly engaging learning experiences. Recently Hornbillfx ranked as the top [VR immersive learning solutions company in the USA](Choosing engaging and immersive learning experiences can give your corporate eLearning the cost-effective edge.
Every time we meet clients who are looking for alternate ways to deliver their training, they often start with the observation that their existing training method — instructor-led training (ILT) or ‘Chalk and Talk’ — isn’t ideal. They say that it’s tedious to organize, learners don’t have the opportunity for follow-ups /clarification later, and that retention is not optimal. But the most pressing concern with ILT is the meagre return on investment. In fact, a client once said, “Often we feel like we don’t get anything tangible for the investment we make. The learning dissolves into thin air, and we are often left with nothing to go back to”.

So, why should you consider immersive eLearning?
Let’s start with the bottom line: It is significantly less expensive to execute; almost free to scale. Think of it this way, once ready, the replication cost of immersive eLearning is minimal; no additional expense on the venue, logistics, trainer, etc.

No end to learning: With immersive eLearning, your employees get proactive learning solutions that are well integrated and logically mapped to the learner's understanding of the concepts. They can learn, revise and re-learn at no additional production cost — your employees can learn as many times as they need.

No loss on the blind side: With immersive eLearning, you can measure your employees’ learning. good measurement enables you to re-skill, up-skill, assign, and reassign your employees based on what they are best suited for.

No loss of learning due to inconsistency: Immersive eLearning offers consistency (bordering on exactitude) of training delivery — even if you get the same trainer to do the ILT every time, you can’t be sure of consistency, can you?

No loss of time in waiting: Immersive eLearning offers inexpensive, convenient scaling. There is no need to worry about the minimum class size to meet ROI; no loss of productive time in waiting. Your employees can be instantly trained and deployed.

When we say cost-efficient, we don’t mean that immersive learning solution is cheap. It does come at a price. Hornbill FX is one of the best 3d immersive learning solutions companies in India. We offer turnkey immersive eLearning solutions, using a combination of 3D animation, 2D animation, Augmented Reality, Virtual Reality, and well-honed principles of instructional design to create truly engaging learning experiences. Recently Hornbillfx ranked as the top VR immersive learning solutions company in the USA. However, we are passionately arguing that it will be worth every penny. Do you agree?).

item.title: 外汇机器人的综合视图：它们是如何工作的 | Comprehensive View of Forex Robots: How They Work
item.description:
随着 COVID-19 大流行的蔓延，大多数公司和企业转而通过在线网络在家工作。 通过缩放会议和电话，远程工作成为可能。 这也让我们了解到，如果我们正确地利用技术的潜力，我们就能创造奇迹！

外汇市场的交易也是如此。 近年来，在线交易已经广为人知，但在这次大流行期间它变得非常有效。 无论您是手动交易者还是熟练的在线交易者，使用机器人进行交易成为在市场交易同时保持安全的唯一可行方式。

但是很长一段时间以来，交易者一直对使用外汇机器人持怀疑态度，而这种突然需要使用它让大多数手动交易者感到困惑。 我们知道，要成功挖掘这些潜力，需要进行大量研究。

在本文中，我们将全面介绍外汇机器人及其工作原理。 除此之外，我们将列出一些利弊以及它对交易的影响。 那么，让我们开始吧，好吗？

外汇机器人的基础知识：

就像任何计算机程序一样，这些机械交易员也是一种计算机程序，但比您的普通话要先进一点。 而手动交易则完全消除了交易时对这些因素的需求。

这些程序旨在帮助您通过一组交易信号在某个时间点做出买卖货币对的决定。

您阅读的大多数文章会让您相信您可以完全依赖机器人，但事实并非如此。 为了确保您获得最大的利润，您需要不断地监督他们并做出改变。 因此，向手动交易者大声疾呼，高效交易仍然需要您的专业知识！

外汇机器人的工作：

谈到外汇机器人，大多数人认为这是一种简单的赚钱方式，但事实并非如此。 这些技术奇迹是使用复杂的自定义脚本和技术指标开发的，可帮助他们扫描图表以找到买入或卖出机会。 程序员投入了无数时间为市场开发最好的软件产品。

为了有效地使用这些，您需要了解出色的交易策略。 如果您认为您可以通过外汇机器人轻松赚钱，请三思而后行！

获取外汇机器人：

现在有两种方法可以利用机器人的好处 • 您可以从开发人员那里购买

• Or you can build it on your own • 或者您可以自己构建它



原文:

Following the spread of the COVID-19 pandemic, most companies and businesses switched to working from home over online networks. With zoom meetings and calls, working from a distance was made possible. This also gave us an insight into the fact that if we harness the potential of technology correctly, we can do wonders!

The same has been true for trading in the forex markets. Online trading had been making its presence known in recent years, but it became extremely effective during this pandemic. Whether you were a manual trader or a proficient online trader, trading using bots became the only viable way to trade in the market while staying safe.

But for a long time, traders have been skeptical about using forex robots and this sudden need of using it left most of the manual traders bewildered. We understand that to successfully extract the potential of these, a substantial amount of research needs to be done.

In this article, we are giving you a comprehensive view of forex robots and how they work. In addition to this, we will be listing some pros and cons along with the effects it will have on trading. So, let’s start, shall we?

The Basics of Forex Robots:

Just like any computer program, these mechanical traders are also a type of computer program but a little more advanced than your average word. While manual trading completely remove the need for those factors while trading.

These programs are meant to help you take decisions to buy or sell currency pairs at a certain point in time with a set of trading signals.

Most of the articles that you read will make you believe that you can completely rely on the robots, but such is not the case. To ensure that you gain maximum profits, you need to constantly supervise them and make changes. So, a huge shout out to the manual traders, your expertise will still be needed for efficient trading!

Working of Forex Robots:

When it comes to forex robots, most people think it’s an easy way to make money, but it’s not. These technological marvels are developed with complex custom scripts and technical indicators that help them scan the charts to find a buy or sell opportunity. Programmers put an innumerable amount of time into developing the best software products for the market.

To efficiently use these, you will need the knowledge of excellent trading strategies. If you think that you can make easy money with forex bots, give it a second thought!

Get a Forex Robot:

Now there are two ways in which you can avail of the benefits of using a bot • Either you can buy it from a developer

• Or you can build it on your own

item.title: 新手开发者打招呼 | Newbie developer says hi
item.description:
大家好！ 我几个月前就开始了这段旅程，但当我加入全栈开发人员时，它开始正式化。 四月训练营💻🥑

首先，我想向您介绍一下我，我的背景以及过去几年在各个领域的表现。

如果非要选择一些形容词来形容自己，我会说我是一个开朗好奇的人。 我今年 30 岁，在我生命的最后 30 年里一直在学习，我学习了不同的学位，我学习了两种语言并尝试了其他四种语言（如阿拉伯语、意大利语、日语），我学习了营销、设计、经济学、艺术、音乐...我喜欢写作、阅读、唱歌和写我自己的歌曲。 我最喜欢的活动是创建新项目和想象新想法。 如果有人问我我的职业是什么，我会说我是一名教师。 自 2012 年以来，我一直在教英语和西班牙语，自 2019 年以来，我也是一名历史和地理老师。教学、领导课程以及寻找/创造创新的方式来解释和工作概念是我最喜欢的活动之一。

这些台词的重点不是炫耀——就我个人而言，我不相信那种将自己的证书或头衔列为自己的定义的愚蠢的现代习惯； 从我的角度来看，一堆文件没有说明一个人的行为或行为——而是描绘了我的整体形象以及我在生活中的表现。 在生活中我不想尝试的事情不多，我不会添加到我的清单中的经历很少。

在西班牙语中，他们会说我是“culo de mal asiento”。 这将意味着像“糟糕的座位对接”。

然而，这种对生活的看法并不符合我认为的生活应有的样子。 人生的目的不能只选择一条路，直到死去； 选择一个套路，一个地方，一个职业，一群人，和其他（第一）世界一样的假期套餐，一样的赚钱体系，一样的结构“结婚+房子+孩子+退休”。 即使我尝试过，我也做不到，因为我需要每天在生活中做不同的事情。

因此，我更感兴趣的领域之一是网络开发：能够自己创建任何数字产品、设计或平台，让我能够启动和推进自己的项目，这让我感到高兴和有足够的动力去掌握新的编程语言、工具包和任何可以让我的专业方式变得更有趣的功能。

Soy HELL0 W0RLD, I'm a fem coder who's gonna nail it! Soy HELL0 W0RLD，我是一个会搞定它的女性编码员！



原文:

Hi everyone! I started this journey months ago, but it started to be official when I joined a Fullstack Dev. bootcamp in April 💻 🥑

First of all, I would like to tell you about me, my background and my performance in diverse fields for the last years.

If I have to choose some adjectives to describe myself, I would say I'm a cheerful and curious. I am 30 and I have been learning for the last 30 years of my life, I studied different degrees, I learnt two languages and tried other four (like Arabic, Italian, Japanese), I have learnt about marketing, design, economics, art, music... I love writing, reading, singing and writing my own songs. My favourite activity is creating new projects and picturing new ideas. If I were asked which is my profession, I would say I'm a teacher. I have been teaching English and Spanish since 2012, I am also a History and Geography teacher since 2019. Teaching, leading a lesson and looking for / creating innovative ways to explain and work concepts is one of the activities I enjoy the most.

The main point of these lines isn't showing off_- personally, I don't believe in that stupid modern habit of listing one's certifications or titles as a definition of themselves; from my point of view, a bunch of papers say nothing about how a person is or acts-_ but picturing a whole image of how I am and how I perform in life. There are not many things I wouldn't like to try in life, there are very few experiences I wouldn't add to my list.

In Spanish, they would say that I am a "culo de mal asiento". This would mean something like "bad seating butt".

However, this vision of life does not fit in what I think life should be. The purpose of human lives can't be choosing just one path to follow until death; choosing a routine, a place, a profession, a group of people, the same holiday package as the rest of the (first) world, the same system to earn money, the same structure "marriage+house+children+retirement". I just can't, even if I tried, because I need to be doing different things in my life, daily.

So one of the fields I have been more interested in was web development: the fact of being able to create by myself any digital product, design or platform which allowed me to launch and boost my own projects makes me happy and motivated enough to master new programming languages, toolkits and any feature that could make my professional way more interesting.

Soy HELL0 W0RLD, I'm a fem coder who's gonna nail it!
