item.title: 构建一个 Svelte 组件以在悬停时绘制 SVG | Build a Svelte Component To Draw SVG On Hover
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我将分享我构建可重用 Svelte 组件以在悬停时绘制 SVG 的过程。 首先尝试减少我的工作项目中的样板文件并发布我的第一个 npm 包。

如果您已经了解以下内容，那就太好了：

这是我的 npm 包： Svelte Hover Draw SVG

欢迎任何反馈！

让我们谈谈动机

在我的工作项目中，我在每个选项卡中都有一个带有内联 SVG 的 Tab 组件：

我喜欢在悬停时添加 SVG 动画，我首先想到的是transition:draw in Svelte：

它有效，但问题如下：

  1. 像on:mouseenter mouseenter 、 {#if cursorOn === 'tab'}和transition:draw这样的样板
  2. 我们需要手动为它们中的每一个添加transition
  3. 当 SVG 位于<SVGInComponent/>类的组件中时，我们不能使用它（ draw只能用于SVGElement或具有getTotalLength()方法的元素。）

我们可以把它们晒干吗？

如果我只是想要这个简单的效果，我不想一次又一次地写样板。
所以我开始寻找一种方法来抽象悬停事件和效果。

我们需要知道的第一件事是draw动画实际上是一个 CSS 过渡：

 // Please refer to https://svelte.dev/tutorial/custom-css-transitions function draw ( node , { delay = 0 , speed , duration , easing : easing$1 = easing . cubicInOut } = {}) { let len = node . getTotalLength (); const style = getComputedStyle ( node ); if ( style . strokeLinecap !== ' butt ' ) { len += parseInt ( style . strokeWidth ); } if ( duration === undefined ) { if ( speed === undefined ) { duration = 800 ; } else { duration = len / speed ; } } else if ( typeof duration === ' function ' ) { duration = duration ( len ); } return { delay , duration , easing : easing$1 , css : ( t , u ) => `stroke-dasharray: ${ t * len } ${ u * len } ` }; }

这是draw的源代码，关键部分是：

 css : ( t , u ) => `stroke-dasharray: ${ t * len } ${ u * len }

当触发transition时，带有自定义@keyframes的 CSS 动画将被添加到该元素：

所以我们要做的很简单：在悬停时切换 CSS 动画！

使绘制 SVG 线条更容易的技巧

我一直在寻找一种方法来简化复杂的@keyframes ，我发现了这篇文章：Chris Coyier的 A Trick That Makes Draw SVG Lines Way Easier 。

通过将pathLength="1"设置为SVGElement （如<path> 、 line 、 rect 、 polyline和polygon ），将stroke-dasharray设置为 1，并在 CSS 中为偏移设置动画！

 <path class= "path" d= "M66.039,133.545 ... " pathLength= "1" />
 .path { stroke-dasharray : 1 ; stroke-dashoffset : 1 ; animation : dash 5s linear alternate infinite ; } @keyframes dash { from { stroke-dashoffset : 1 ; } to { stroke-dashoffset : 0 ; } }

我还发现我可以将这个path类放到内部有 SVG 的元素中，这意味着我们不需要以编程方式将动画添加到SVGElement ：

 <div class= "tabs" > <a href= "#tab1" class= "tab" > <svg stroke-width= "2" > <!-- This works --> <path class= "path" pathLength= "1" d= "..." /> </svg> <span> Tab 1 </span> </a> <!-- This also works --> <a href= "#tab2" class= "tab path" > <svg stroke-width= "2" > <path pathLength= "1" d= "..." /> </svg> <span> Tab 2 </span> </a> </div>

为抽象制作一个包装器组件

创建包装组件的目标：

  • 让包装器组件处理悬停事件和效果。
  • 悬停时触发动画（组件级别，而不仅仅是 SVG）。
  • 用户可以将他们的组件/ SVG 放在包装器组件中。

这是最后的方法：

你可以在这里试试： Svelte REPL

易于使用 + 编写 FTW 的代码更少💪

  • 嵌套元素（显示hovering状态）：

包起来

有一些我在本文中没有提到并最终放弃的技术考虑，比如设计自定义过渡和使用命名槽来验证SVGElement等。

让用户使用duration和easing等原生参数自定义过渡效果会很棒，但如果这种简单的效果可以通过 CSS 来实现，那就太过分了。

就这样。 感谢您的阅读！ 你可以在 Twitter 上找到我： @davipon



原文:

In this post, I'll share my journey of building a reusable Svelte component to draw SVG on hover. Begin by trying to reduce boilerplate in my work project and publishing my first npm package.

It's great if you've already known below things:

This is my npm package: Svelte Hover Draw SVG

Any feedbacks are welcomed!

Let's talk about motivation

In my work project, I have a Tab component with inline SVG in each tab:

I like to add SVG animation on hover, and the first that came to my mind was transition:draw in Svelte:

It works, but here are the problems:

  1. Boilerplates like on:mouseenter, {#if cursorOn === 'tab'}, and transition:draw
  2. SVG might have multiple <path>, line, rect, polyline, polygon. We need to add transition to each of them manually
  3. We can't use it when the SVG is in a component like <SVGInComponent/> (draw can only be used on SVGElement or element which has getTotalLength() method.)

Can we DRY them up?

DRY = don't repeat yourself

I don't want to write boilerplate again and again if I just want this simple effect.
So I started to find a way to abstract the hovering event & effect.

First thing we need to know is the draw animation is actually a CSS transition:

// Please refer to https://svelte.dev/tutorial/custom-css-transitions
function draw(node, { delay = 0, speed, duration, easing: easing$1 = easing.cubicInOut } = {}) { let len = node.getTotalLength(); const style = getComputedStyle(node); if (style.strokeLinecap !== 'butt') { len += parseInt(style.strokeWidth); } if (duration === undefined) { if (speed === undefined) { duration = 800; } else { duration = len / speed; } } else if (typeof duration === 'function') { duration = duration(len); } return { delay, duration, easing: easing$1, css: (t, u) => `stroke-dasharray: ${t * len} ${u * len}` };
}

This is the source code of draw, the key part is:

 css: (t, u) => `stroke-dasharray: ${t * len} ${u * len}

When the transition is triggered, a CSS animation with custom @keyframes would be added to that element:

So what we're trying to do is quite simple: toggling a CSS animation on hover!

A Trick That Makes Drawing SVG Lines Way Easier

I was looking for a way to simplify the complex @keyframes, and I found this article: A Trick That Makes Drawing SVG Lines Way Easier by Chris Coyier.

By setting the pathLength="1" to SVGElement (like <path>, line, rect, polyline, and polygon), set the stroke-dasharray to 1, and animate the offset in CSS!

<path class="path" d="M66.039,133.545 ... " pathLength="1" />

.path { stroke-dasharray: 1; stroke-dashoffset: 1; animation: dash 5s linear alternate infinite;
} @keyframes dash { from { stroke-dashoffset: 1; } to { stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
}

I also found that I can put this path class to elements that have SVG inside, which means we don't need to add the animation to SVGElement programmatically:

<div class="tabs"> <a href="#tab1" class="tab"> <svg stroke-width="2"> <!-- This works --> <path class="path" pathLength="1" d="..." /> </svg> <span>Tab 1</span> </a> <!-- This also works --> <a href="#tab2" class="tab path"> <svg stroke-width="2"> <path pathLength="1" d="..."/> </svg> <span>Tab 2</span> </a>
</div>

Make a wrapper component for abstraction

The goal of creating a wrapper component:

  • Let the wrapper component handle the hover event & effect.
  • Trigger animation on hover (component level, not just SVG).
  • User can just put their components/ SVGs in the wrapper component.

Here is the final approach:

You can try it out here: Svelte REPL

Easy to use + less code to write FTW 💪

  • SVG Component (set draw time to 2s, default is 1s):

  • Nested elements (expose hovering status):

Wrapping up

There were some technical considerations I didn't mention in this article and finally gave up on, like designing a custom transition and using named slots to validate SVGElement, ...etc.

It'd be great to let users customize the transition using the native parameters like duration and easing, but it's overkill if this simple effect can be achieved by CSS.

That's all. Thank you for your reading! 🙌

You can find me on Twitter: @davipon
item.author: davipon

item.title: 带有 Flutter 的 Google Maps Places API | Google Maps Places API With Flutter
item.description:
您好，欢迎回到 Flutter 应用开发教程系列。 在此之前，我们已经制作了启动画面，编写了主题，制作了自定义应用栏，制作了身份验证屏幕，建立了与firebase 云和模拟器的连接，经过身份验证的用户并访问了用户在 firebase 项目上的位置。

关于

作为用户屏幕流程的一部分，我们已经可以访问用户位置。 在这第 10 期中，我们将使用用户的位置来使用 google 的Places API获取最近的 20个寺庙。 我们将使用HTTP包获取位置，然后我们将使用 Firebase 函数编写另一个 HTTPS Callable 以将这些寺庙存储在 Firestore 中。 由于我们使用的是 API 密钥，因此我们将使用flutter_dotenv包对其保密。 从这里找到源代码以开始本节。

套餐

点环境

所以，首先让我们安装flutter_dotenv包。

 flutter pub add flutter_dotenv

在项目的根目录下创建一个 .env 文件。

在 .gitignore 文件中添加 .env 文件。

在 main.dart 文件的 main() 方法中初始化 .env 文件。

 import 'package:flutter_dotenv/flutter_dotenv.dart'; void main() async { .... // Initialize dot env await dotenv.load(fileName: ".env"); // Pass prefs as value in MyApp runApp(MyApp(prefs: prefs)); }

我们还需要将 .env 文件添加到pubspec.yaml文件的 assets 部分。

 #find assets section assets: # Splash Screens - assets/splash/om_splash.png - assets/splash/om_lotus_splash.png - assets/splash/om_lotus_splash_1152x1152.png # Onboard Screens - assets/onboard/FindTemples.png - assets/onboard/FindVenues.png # Auth Screens - assets/AuthScreen/WelcomeScreenImage_landscape_2.png // add this here # Dotenv flie - .env

谷歌地图位置 API

要使用 google places API，我们需要 places API 密钥。 为此，请前往谷歌云控制台，设置结算账户，并为 Goog Places API 创建一个新的 API 密钥。 然后在 .env 文件中添加该 API 密钥。

安装HTTP包。

无需对 HTTP 包进行额外设置。

文件夹结构

与 Home 和 Auth 文件夹一样，temples 目录将包含与 Temples 关联的所有文件。 因此，让我们创建各种文件和文件夹，用于获取和显示寺庙。

 # Make folders mkdir lib/screens/temples lib/screens/temples/providers lib/screens/temples/screens lib/screens/temples/widgets lib/screens/temples/models lib/screens/temples/utils # Make files touch lib/screens/temples/providers/temples_provider.dart lib/screens/temples/screens/temples_screen.dart lib/screens/temples/widgets/temples_item_widget.dart lib/screens/temples/models/temple.dart lib/screens/temples/utils/temple_utils.dart

与之前的博客一样，我们将所有应用程序逻辑保存在提供程序文件中。 最重要的是，我们还需要一个 utils 文件来存储我们将在提供程序类上使用的一些函数。 所以，首先，让我们创建两个简单的 Temple_utils.dart 函数

实用程序

从 .env 文件导入 API 密钥。

  • 当我们使用rankby时，类型参数是必需的。
  • 所以，使用其他东西作为建立类型。 另一种方法是一个简单的映射器，它的唯一目的是将传入列表映射到 TempleModels 列表（我们将在接下来创建）并将其返回。 这将使我们以后的代码更加简洁。

     List<TempleModel> mapper(List results) { final newList = results .map( (temple) => TempleModel( name: temple['name'], address: temple['address'], latLng: LatLng( temple['latLng']['lat'], temple['latLng']['lon'], ), imageUrl: temple['imageRef'], placesId: temple['place_id'], ), ) .toList(); return newList; }

    寺庙模型

    在模型内部的 Temple.dart 文件中，让我们创建一个寺庙模型。

     import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart'; class TempleModel { // name of temple final String name; // the address final String address; // geo location final LatLng latLng; // ImageUrls final String imageUrl; // id given to each item by places api final String placesId; const TempleModel( {required this.name, required this.address, required this.latLng, required this.imageUrl, required this.placesId}); }

    将保存在 Firestore 中的每个寺庙都将有一个名称、地址、地理坐标、imageUrl 和一个由 google 的 place API 提供的 ID。

    带有 HTTP 和提供程序的 Google Place API

    现在，是时候编写一个提供程序类来负责获取附近的寺庙列表了。 这将是一个很长的文件，需要解释很多事情。 因此，我们将从上到下逐个代码。

    导入模块并创建提供程序类。

     import 'dart:convert'; import 'dart:math'; import 'package:cloud_firestore/cloud_firestore.dart'; import 'package:cloud_functions/cloud_functions.dart'; import 'package:firebase_auth/firebase_auth.dart'; import 'package:firebase_storage/firebase_storage.dart' as firbase_storage; import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart'; import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart'; import 'package:http/http.dart' as http; //custom modules import 'package:temple/screens/temples/models/temple.dart'; import 'package:temple/screens/temples/utils/temple_utils.dart'; class TempleProvider with ChangeNotifier {}

    在类中，实例化我们将使用的 Firebase 产品。

     // Instantiate FIbrebase products final FirebaseAuth auth = FirebaseAuth.instance; final FirebaseFunctions functions = FirebaseFunctions.instance; final FirebaseFirestore firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance; // Estabish sotrage instance for bucket of our choice // once e mulator runs you can find the bucket name at storage tab final firbase_storage.FirebaseStorage storage = firbase_storage.FirebaseStorage.instanceFor( bucket: 'astha-being-hindu-tutorial.appspot.com');

    对于Firebase 存储，我们不会使用默认存储桶来存储将获取其 URL 以保存在 Firestore 上的图像。 那么，这个逻辑是怎么回事？ 您会看到 google places API 不提供图片，它是由details api提供的。 我们不会使用它。 但相反，我有一些从Unsplash下载的随机（4 个）印度教图像，我将其存储在存储中并在图像中获取随机URL 并将其分配给寺庙模型。 您不必这样做并为imageRef提供硬编码的图像 URL，但它是为了练习读取存储。

    其他字段和吸气剂

    // Instantiate Temple Utils final TemplesUtils templesUtils = TemplesUtils(); // Create the fake list of temples List<TempleModel>? _temples = []; // User location from db LatLng? _userLocation; // Getters List<TempleModel> get temples => [..._temples as List]; LatLng get userLocation => _userLocation as LatLng; // List of Images static const List<String> imagePaths = [ 'image_1.jpg', 'image_2.jpg', 'image_3.jpg', 'image_4.jpg', ];

    未来返回地点 API 结果

    // Future method to get temples Future<List<TempleModel>?> getNearyByTemples(LatLng userLocation) async { // Get urls from the temple utils // #1 Uri url = templesUtils.searchUrl(userLocation); try { // Set up references for firebase products. // Callable getNearbyTemples // #2 HttpsCallable getNearbyTemples = functions.httpsCallable('getNearbyTemples'); // Collection reference for temples // # 3 CollectionReference templeDocRef = firestore.collection('temples'); // Get one doc from temples collection // #4 QuerySnapshot querySnapshot = await templeDocRef.limit(1).get(); // A reference to a folder in storage that has images. // #5 firbase_storage.Reference storageRef = storage.ref('TempleImages'); // We'll only get nearby temples if the temple's collection empty // #6 if (querySnapshot.docs.isEmpty) { print("Temple collection is empty"); // get the result from api search // #7 final res = await http.get(url); // decode to json result // #8 final decodedRes = await jsonDecode(res.body) as Map; // get result as list // #9 final results = await decodedRes['results'] as List; // Get random image url from available ones to put as images // Since we have 4 images we'll get 0-3 values from Random() // #10 final imgUrl = await storageRef .child(imagePaths[Random().nextInt(4)]) .getDownloadURL(); // Call the function // #11 final templesListCall = await getNearbyTemples.call(<String, dynamic>{ 'templeList': [...results], 'imageRef': imgUrl, }); // map the templesList returned by https callable // we'll use utils mapper here // #12 final newTempleLists = templesUtils.mapper(templesListCall.data['temples']); // update the new temples list // #13 _temples = [...newTempleLists]; } else { // If the temples collection already has temples then we won't write // but just fetch temples collection // #14 print("Temple collection is not empty"); try { // get all temples documents final tempSnapShot = await templeDocRef.get(); // fetch the values as list. final tempList = tempSnapShot.docs[0]['temples'] as List; // map the results into a list final templesList = templesUtils.mapper(tempList); // update temples _temples = [...templesList]; } catch (e) { // incase of error temples list in empty // # 15 _temples = []; } } } catch (e) { // incase of error temples list in empty _temples = []; } // notify all the listeners notifyListeners(); // #16 return _temples; }

    好的，现在我们在这篇博客“getNearyByTemples”中所做的所有工作的主要方法已经创建。 让我们按数字：

    1. 使用我们之前创建的 Temple 工具来获取可供 HTTP 包使用的 URL。

    2. 对 HTTP 可调用getNearyByTemples的引用，我们将在此提供程序会话之后创建它。 它负责保存我们在此搜索期间获取的所有寺庙的列表。

    3. 参考寺庙收藏。

    4. 对存储桶内名为“TempleImages”的文件夹的引用。

    5. 我们正在检查我们之前获取的寺庙文档是否为空。 逻辑是我们不想在每次用户使用我们的应用程序时调用 Place Api、Firestores 和 Functions。 如果寺庙的收藏为空或不存在，我们只会获取寺庙并将它们保存在 FireStore 上。

    6. 您可以使用 postman 之类的软件或仅使用 chrome 浏览器来查看 get 请求的结果。

    7. 我们将把它提取为 List 类型。

    8. 我们随机下载一个 URL 并将其分配给我们的 HTTPS 可调用文件中所需的 imageRef 属性。

    9. 我们现在称我们的 HTTPS 可调用，提供寺庙列表和图像的 Url。 它会将列表保存在 Firesotre 的 Temples 集合中并返回该列表。

    10. 返回的列表现在将用于使用 Temple Utils 的映射器方法更新我们的 Temple 模型列表。

    11. 我们的应用程序将使用相同的列表在寺庙屏幕上显示精美的寺庙卡片列表。

    12. 在这种情况下，与 if 块不同，我们不从 API 获取寺庙列表，我们只是读取保存在寺庙集合中的所有文档。 之后这个过程和上面一样。

    13. 如果出现错误，寺庙列表将为空。

    14. 返回这个新列表非常重要。 我们需要这个 List 作为由 FutureBuilder 获取的 QuerySnapshot 数据，以便在我们的应用程序上显示它。

    使用 Firebase 函数在 FireStore 上编写 Google Places API 搜索结果

    在index.js文件中，我们现在将创建另一个 HTTPS 可调用函数“getNearbyTemples”。 此方法将创建一个包含寺庙对象列表的数组，然后将其保存到寺庙集合中。

     exports.getNearbyTemples = functions.https.onCall(async (data, _) => { try { // Notify function's been called functions.logger.log("Add nearby temples function was called"); // Create array of temple objects. let temples = data.templeList.map((temple) => { return { 'place_id': temple['place_id'], 'address': temple['vicinity'] ? temple['vicinity'] : 'Not Available', 'name': temple['name'] ? temple['name'] : 'Not Available', 'latLng': { 'lat': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lat'] : 'Not Available', 'lon': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lng'] : 'Not Available', 'dateAdded': admin.firestore.Timestamp.now() }, 'imageRef': data.imageRef } } ); // save the temples array to temples collection as one document named temples await db.collection('temples').add({ temples: temples }); } catch (e) { // if error return errormsg return { 'Error Msg': e }; } // If everything's fine return the temples array. return temples; });

    我没有为此操作分配内存。 这是非常棘手和耗时的。 如果你愿意，你可以尝试。 所以，我们的列表至少对于这个应用程序永远不会超过 20。因此，在 Temple 的集合中，我们保存的不是 20 个文档，而是一个包含 20 个项目的文档作为字段“temples”， db.collection('temples')。添加（{寺庙：寺庙}） 。

    使用 Firestore 触发器处理更新

    假设用户更改了位置或新的寺庙已添加到 google 数据库中。 它应该反映在 Firestore Temples 系列中。 但是我们应该谨慎处理更新，只有在旧文档有任何更改时才编写新文档。 对于我们的寺庙集合，我们可以只匹配places_id，并且只采取相应的行动。 现在，让我们在index.js上编写一些代码。

     // When the temple List Updates exports.updateNearbyTemples = functions.runWith({ timeoutSeconds: 120, memory: "256MB" }).firestore.document('temples/{id}').onUpdate(async (change, context) => { // If theres both new and old value if (change.before.exists && change.after.exists) { // temples list both new and old let newTemplesList = change.after.data()['temples']; let oldTemplesList = change.before.data()['temples']; // Places Id list from both new and old list let oldTemplesIdList = oldTemplesList.map(temple => temple['place_id']); let newTemplesIdList = newTemplesList.map(temple => temple['place_id']); // Lets find out if theres new temples id by filtering with old one let filteredList = newTemplesIdList.filter(x => !oldTemplesIdList.includes(x)); // if the length are not same of fileted list has //length of 0 then nothing new is there so just return if (oldTemplesIdList.length != newTemplesIdList.length || filteredList.length == 0) { functions.logger.log("Nothing is changed so onUpdate returned"); return; } // If somethings changed then try { functions.logger.log("On Update was called "); // Make new list of temples let temples = newTemplesList.map((temple) => { return { 'place_id': temple['place_id'], 'address': temple['vicinity'] ? temple['vicinity'] : 'Not Available', 'name': temple['name'] ? temple['name'] : 'Not Available', 'latLng': { 'lat': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lat'] : 'Not Available', 'lon': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lng'] : 'Not Available', 'dateAdded': admin.firestore.Timestamp.now() } } } ); // use the current context id to update temples, no need to merge await db.collection('temples').doc(context.params.id).set({ 'palces_id_list': newTemplesIdList, temples: temples }); } catch (e) { throw e; } return { 'status': 200 }; } // return nothing return null; });

    通过此更新，该功能将不会在用户每次加载太阳穴屏幕时运行。 它会在生产模式上为我们节省很多钱。

    使 Provider 类可用于小部件

    现在，我们的班级已经准备好工作了。 因此，让我们通过更新app.dart文件中的 MultiProviders 列表来使它们可用。

     MultiProvider( providers: [ ... ChangeNotifierProvider(create: (context) => TempleProvider()), ... ], ...

    现在，MultiProviders 的所有后代都可以访问 GetNearbyTemples 方法。 那么，我们究竟要在哪里调用这个方法呢？ 那么在下一篇博客中，我们会让我们的主页看起来更好看一点。 在该主页上，将有一个指向寺庙列表屏幕的链接。 单击链接时将执行 In 方法。 现在，让我们在偏离博客的主题之前结束这个博客。

    概括

    在我们离开之前，让我们总结一下到目前为止我们所做的事情。

    1. 我们创建了 google maps places API。
    2. 通过安装 Flutter_DotEnv 包，我们保护了 API 不被公开。
    3. 还添加了 HTTP 包，它在从 API 获取寺庙列表方面发挥了至关重要的作用。
    4. 我们创建了一个实用程序文件，但只有一个实用程序。 稍后，如果你愿意，你可以写一个从用户到寺庙的距离计算器方法来表示在谷歌地图中。
    5. 然后，我们在提供程序类中编写了一个方法，该方法获取一个搜索列表，并将其传递给 firebase 云函数。
    6. 如果集合为空，firebase 函数会将寺庙列表保存到 Firestore。
    7. 然后我们编写了一个更新触发器，它只会在值更改时运行。

    最终代码

    显示支持

    好了，这次就到此为止。 这个系列，还没完，下次上传我们重新设计我们丑陋的平凡主页，创建一个寺庙列表屏幕并执行我们今天创建的方法

    所以，请喜欢，评论，并与您的朋友分享文章。 感谢您抽出宝贵时间以及订阅博客时事通讯的人，我们对此表示感谢。 继续支持我们。 这是来自 Khadka's Coding Lounge 的 Nibesh，这是一家制作网站和移动应用程序的自由职业者。



    • 原文:

    Hello and welcome back to Flutter App Dev Tutorial Series. Before this, we have already made a splash screen, wrote a theme, made a custom app bar, made authentication screen, set up a connection with firebase cloud and emulators, authenticated users and accessed user's location on firebase projects.

    About

    As a part of the user-screen flow, we already have access to the user location. On this 10th installment, we'll use the user's location to fetch the nearest twenty temples using google's Places API. We'll fetch places with the HTTP package, then we'll write another HTTPS Callable with Firebase Functions to store those temples in Firestore. Since we're using an API key, we'll use the flutter_dotenv package to keep it secret. Find the source code to start this section from here.

    Packages

    DotEnv

    So, first lets install flutter_dotenv package.

    flutter pub add flutter_dotenv

    Create a .env file at the root of your project.

    Add the .env file in .gitignore file.

    Initialize .env file in main() method of our main.dart file.

    import 'package:flutter_dotenv/flutter_dotenv.dart';

void main() async {
....

 // Initialize dot env
  await dotenv.load(fileName: ".env");
// Pass prefs as value in MyApp
  runApp(MyApp(prefs: prefs));
}

    We also need to add the .env file to the assets section of the pubspec.yaml file.

    #find assets section 
assets:
    # Splash Screens
    - assets/splash/om_splash.png
    - assets/splash/om_lotus_splash.png
    - assets/splash/om_lotus_splash_1152x1152.png
    # Onboard Screens
    - assets/onboard/FindTemples.png
    - assets/onboard/FindVenues.png
    # Auth Screens
    - assets/AuthScreen/WelcomeScreenImage_landscape_2.png  
// add this here
   # Dotenv flie
    - .env

    Google Maps Places API

    To use the google places API we'll need the places API key. For that please go to google cloud console, set up a billing account, and create a new API key for Goog Places API. Then add that API key in the .env file.

    .env

    #Without quotes
GMAP_PLACES_API_KEY = Your_API_KEY

    HTTP

    Install the HTTP package.

    There's no need for extra setup with the HTTP package.

    Folder Structures

    Like Home and Auth Folders, the temples directory will have all the files associated with temples. So, let's create various files and folders we'll use to fetch & display temples.

    # Make folders
mkdir  lib/screens/temples lib/screens/temples/providers lib/screens/temples/screens lib/screens/temples/widgets lib/screens/temples/models lib/screens/temples/utils

# Make files
touch lib/screens/temples/providers/temples_provider.dart lib/screens/temples/screens/temples_screen.dart  lib/screens/temples/widgets/temples_item_widget.dart lib/screens/temples/models/temple.dart lib/screens/temples/utils/temple_utils.dart

    Like the blogs before it, we'll keep our all apps logic inside the provider file. On top of that, we'll also need a utils file to store a few functions that we'll use on the provider class. So, first, let's create two simple functions of temple_utils.dart

    Utilities

    temple_utils.dart

    import 'package:flutter_dotenv/flutter_dotenv.dart';
import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart';

class TemplesUtils {
  // Base url for google maps nearbysearch
  // #1
  static const String _baseUrlNearBySearch =
      "https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/place/nearbysearch/json?";
  // get api key
  // #2
  final String _placesApi = dotenv.env['GMAP_PLACES_API_KEY'] as String;

  // Create a method that'll parse complete url and return it using http package
  // #3
  Uri searchUrl(LatLng userLocation) {
    // Create variables that'll pass maps API parmas as string
  // # 4
//===================//
    final api = "&key=$_placesApi";

    final location =
        "location=${userLocation.latitude},${userLocation.longitude}";

    const type = "&type=hindu_temple";
    // Closest first
  // #5
    const rankBy = "&rankby=distance";
//=====================//
    // Parse URL to get a new uri object
  // #6
    final url =
        Uri.parse(_baseUrlNearBySearch + location + rankBy + type + api);

    return URL;
  }
}
    1. Google Maps NearbySearch takes several parameters, among them we'll use location(required parameter), rankby and type .
    2. Import API key from .env file.
    3. Search Url is a function that'll take user location then, combine the base URL acceptable parameter by search API and return parsed URI.
    4. The API key is a must, on every URL to get a result back from API.
    5. The rankby="distance" sorts the search results based on distance. When we're using rankby the type parameter is required.
    6. The final URL to be used by the HTTP package to search for temples.

    *Note: If you're from a place that doesn't have temples(some or not at all) you probably won't see any results. So, use something else for the establishment type. *

    Another method will be a simple mapper, its sole purpose is to map the incoming list into a list of TempleModels(which we'll create next) and return it as such. This will make our code later much cleaner.

     List<TempleModel> mapper(List results) {
    final newList = results
        .map(
          (temple) => TempleModel(
            name: temple['name'],
            address: temple['address'],
            latLng: LatLng(
              temple['latLng']['lat'],
              temple['latLng']['lon'],
            ),
            imageUrl: temple['imageRef'],
            placesId: temple['place_id'],
          ),
        )
        .toList();

    return newList;
  }

    Temple Model

    The Temple model class will define a framework for the information to be stored about the temple. On the temple.dart file inside models let's create a temple model.

    import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart';

class TempleModel {
  // name of temple
  final String name;
  // the address
  final String address;
  // geo location
  final LatLng latLng;
  // ImageUrls
  final String imageUrl;
  // id given to each item by places api
  final String placesId;

  const TempleModel(
      {required this.name,
      required this.address,
      required this.latLng,
      required this.imageUrl,
      required this.placesId});
}

    Each temple that'll be saved in Firestore will have a name, address, geographical coordinates, imageUrl, and an ID given by google's place API.

    Google Place API with HTTP and Provider

    Now, it's time to write a provider class that'll take care of fetching the nearby temples list. This will be a long file with many things to explain. So, we'll go piece by piece codes from top to bottom.

    Import Modules and Create a provider class. 

    
import 'dart:convert';
import 'dart:math';
import 'package:cloud_firestore/cloud_firestore.dart';
import 'package:cloud_functions/cloud_functions.dart';
import 'package:firebase_auth/firebase_auth.dart';
import 'package:firebase_storage/firebase_storage.dart' as firbase_storage;
import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart';
import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart';
import 'package:http/http.dart' as http;

//custom modules
import 'package:temple/screens/temples/models/temple.dart';
import 'package:temple/screens/temples/utils/temple_utils.dart';

class TempleProvider with ChangeNotifier {}

    Inside the class, instantiate Firebase Products we will be using. 

    // Instantiate FIbrebase products
  final FirebaseAuth auth = FirebaseAuth.instance;
  final FirebaseFunctions functions = FirebaseFunctions.instance;
  final FirebaseFirestore firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance;
  // Estabish sotrage instance for bucket of our choice
  // once e mulator runs you can find the bucket name at storage tab
  final firbase_storage.FirebaseStorage storage =
      firbase_storage.FirebaseStorage.instanceFor(
          bucket: 'astha-being-hindu-tutorial.appspot.com');

    With the Firebase Storage we will not use a default bucket to store images whose URL will be fetched to save on Firestore. So, what's up with this logic? You see google places API doesn't provide images, it's provided by details api. We'll not be using it. But instead, I have some random(4 in numbers) Hindu images that I downloaded from Unsplash, which I'll store in storage and fetch a random URL among images and assign it to the temple model. You don't have to do it and provide the hardcoded image URL for imageRef, but it's to practice reading storage.

    Other Fields and Getters 

    // Instantiate Temple Utils
final TemplesUtils templesUtils = TemplesUtils();
  // Create the fake list of temples
  List<TempleModel>? _temples = [];
  // User location from db
  LatLng? _userLocation;

// Getters
  List<TempleModel> get temples => [..._temples as List];
  LatLng get userLocation => _userLocation as LatLng;

// List of Images

  static const List<String> imagePaths = [
    'image_1.jpg',
    'image_2.jpg',
    'image_3.jpg',
    'image_4.jpg',
  ];

    The imagePaths is a list of literally the name of images that I've uploaded in a folder named "TempleImages" inside our bucket we referenced earlier in Emulator's Storage.

    Future To Return Places API Result 

    
// Future method to get temples
   Future<List<TempleModel>?> getNearyByTemples(LatLng userLocation) async {
    // Get urls from the temple utils
    // #1
    Uri url = templesUtils.searchUrl(userLocation);

    try {
      // Set up references for firebase products.
      // Callable getNearbyTemples
       // #2
      HttpsCallable getNearbyTemples =
          functions.httpsCallable('getNearbyTemples');
      // Collection reference for temples
      // # 3
      CollectionReference templeDocRef = firestore.collection('temples');
      // Get one doc from temples collection
      // #4
      QuerySnapshot querySnapshot = await templeDocRef.limit(1).get();
      // A reference to a folder in storage that has images.
      // #5
      firbase_storage.Reference storageRef = storage.ref('TempleImages');

      // We'll only get nearby temples if the temple's collection empty
      // #6
      if (querySnapshot.docs.isEmpty) {
        print("Temple collection is empty");
        // get the result from api search
       // #7
        final res = await http.get(url);
        // decode to json result
       // #8
        final decodedRes = await jsonDecode(res.body) as Map;
        // get result as list
       // #9
        final results = await decodedRes['results'] as List;
        // Get random image url from available ones to put as images
        // Since we have 4 images we'll get 0-3 values from Random()
       // #10
        final imgUrl = await storageRef
            .child(imagePaths[Random().nextInt(4)])
            .getDownloadURL();
        // Call the function
       // #11
        final templesListCall = await getNearbyTemples.call(<String, dynamic>{
          'templeList': [...results],
          'imageRef': imgUrl,
        });

        // map the templesList returned by https callable
       // we'll use utils mapper here
       // #12
        final newTempleLists = templesUtils.mapper(templesListCall.data['temples']);
        // update the new temples list
       // #13
        _temples = [...newTempleLists];
      } else {
        // If the temples collection already has temples then we won't write
        // but just fetch temples collection
       // #14
        print("Temple collection is not empty");
        try {
          // get all temples documents
          final tempSnapShot = await templeDocRef.get();
          // fetch the values as list.
          final tempList = tempSnapShot.docs[0]['temples'] as List;
          // map the results into a list
          final templesList = templesUtils.mapper(tempList);
          // update temples
          _temples = [...templesList];
        } catch (e) {
          // incase of error temples list in empty
       // # 15
          _temples = [];
        }
      }
    } catch (e) {
      // incase of error temples list in empty
      _temples = [];
    }
// notify all the listeners
    notifyListeners();
 // #16
  return _temples;
  }

    Alright, now the main method that'll do everything we've worked on so far in this blog "getNearyByTemples" has been created. Let's go by numbers:

    1. User the temple utils we created earlier to get the URL ready to be used by the HTTP package.

    2. Reference to the HTTP callable getNearyByTemples, which we'll create after this provider session. It's responsible to save the list of all the temples we fetch during this search.

    3. Reference to temple collection.

    4. Temple reference be used to read a single document from the collection.

    5. References to a folder named "TempleImages" inside the bucket of storage.

    6. We're checking if the temple doc we fetched earlier is empty. The logic is that we don't want to call for Place Api, Firestores, and Functions every time user uses our app. We'll only fetch temples and save them on FireStore if the temple's collection is empty or doesn't exist.

    7. HTTP get() method can be used to fetch the results. You can use software like postman or just a chrome browser to see the results of the get request.

    8. Json Decode Function parses strings to json data types.

    9. Places API provides the response of a list of temples as a list with results as its key. We'll extract that as the List type.

    10. Firebase Storage provided the means to download URL from the reference. We're randomly downloading a URL and assigning it to imageRef property needed in our HTTPS callable.

    11. We call our HTTPS callable now, provide with temples list and image's Url. It'll save the list in Firesotre's Temples collection and return that list.

    12. The returned list will be now used to update our List of Temple Models using the mapper method of Temple Utils.

    13. This same list will be used by our app to display a beautiful list of temple cards on the temples screen.

    14. The else block only executes if the temple's collection already has a list of temples. In that case unlike in if block we do not fetch the temples list from API, we just read all the documents that are saved in the temple's collection. After this process is the same as above.

    15. In case of errors, the temples list will be empty.

    16. It is very important to return this new list. We will need this List as QurerySnapshot data fetched by FutureBuilder to display it on our app.

    Write Google Places API Search Results On FireStore With Firebase Functions

    Inside the index.js file we'll now create another HTTPS callable function "getNearbyTemples". This method will create an array with the list of temple objects and then save it to the temples collection.

    exports.getNearbyTemples = functions.https.onCall(async (data, _) => {

    try {
        // Notify function's been called
        functions.logger.log("Add nearby temples function was called");
        // Create array of temple objects.
        let temples = data.templeList.map((temple) => {
            return {
                'place_id': temple['place_id'],
                'address': temple['vicinity'] ? temple['vicinity'] : 'Not Available',
                'name': temple['name'] ? temple['name'] : 'Not Available',
                'latLng': {
                    'lat': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lat'] : 'Not Available', 'lon': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lng'] : 'Not Available',
                    'dateAdded': admin.firestore.Timestamp.now()
                },
                'imageRef': data.imageRef
            }
        }

        );

        // save the temples array to temples collection as one document named temples 
        await db.collection('temples').add({ temples: temples });

    } catch (e) {
        // if error return errormsg
        return { 'Error Msg': e };
    }
    // If everything's fine return the temples array.
    return temples;
});

    I have not allocated memory for this operation. It was very tricky and time-consuming. If you want you can experiment. A firebase document can store up to 1MB in size. So, our list at least for this app will never grow beyond 20. So, inside the temple's collection, we are not saving 20 documents but one document with 20 items as a field "temples", db.collection('temples').add({ temples: temples }).

    Handle Updates With Firestore Triggers

    Let's say the user changed location or a new temple has been added to the google database. It should be reflected in the Firestore Temples collection. But we should handle updates carefully and only write new documents if there are any changes to the old ones. For our temples collection, we can just match the places_id, and only take action accordingly. Firebase provides onUpdate() trigger to handle this type of work. Now, let's write some code on the index.js.

    
// When the temple List Updates
exports.updateNearbyTemples = functions.runWith({
    timeoutSeconds: 120,
    memory: "256MB"
}).firestore.document('temples/{id}').onUpdate(async (change, context) => {
    // If theres both new and old value
    if (change.before.exists && change.after.exists) {
        // temples list both new and old
        let newTemplesList = change.after.data()['temples'];
        let oldTemplesList = change.before.data()['temples'];

        // Places Id list from both new and old list
        let oldTemplesIdList = oldTemplesList.map(temple => temple['place_id']);
        let newTemplesIdList = newTemplesList.map(temple => temple['place_id']);

        // Lets find out if theres new temples id by filtering with old one
        let filteredList = newTemplesIdList.filter(x => !oldTemplesIdList.includes(x));

        // if the length are not same of fileted list has 
        //length of 0 then nothing new is there so just return
        if (oldTemplesIdList.length != newTemplesIdList.length || filteredList.length == 0) {
            functions.logger.log("Nothing is changed so onUpdate returned");
            return;
        }
        // If somethings changed then 
        try {
            functions.logger.log("On Update was called ");
            // Make new list of temples
            let temples = newTemplesList.map((temple) => {
                return {
                    'place_id': temple['place_id'],
                    'address': temple['vicinity'] ? temple['vicinity'] : 'Not Available',
                    'name': temple['name'] ? temple['name'] : 'Not Available',
                    'latLng': {
                        'lat': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lat'] : 'Not Available', 'lon': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lng'] : 'Not Available',
                        'dateAdded': admin.firestore.Timestamp.now()
                    }
                }
            }
            );
            // use the current context id to update temples, no need to merge
            await db.collection('temples').doc(context.params.id).set({ 'palces_id_list': newTemplesIdList, temples: temples });
        }
        catch (e) { throw e; }
        return { 'status': 200 };
    }
    // return nothing
    return null;
});

    Changes.before.data gives values already in the Firestore and changes.after.data gives value newly gotten from the API. With this update, the function will not run every time the user loads the temples screen. It will save us lots of money on production mode.

    Making Provider Class available for Widgets

    Now, our classes are ready for work. So, let's make them available by updating the MultiProviders list in the app.dart file.

     MultiProvider(
      providers: [
       ...
        ChangeNotifierProvider(create: (context) => TempleProvider()),
        ...
      ],
...

    Now, the GetNearbyTemples method is accessible for all the descendants of MultiProviders. So, where exactly are we going to call this method? Well in the next blog, We'll make our home page a little bit better looking. On that homepage, there will be a link to Temple List Screen. In method will be executed when the link is clicked. For now, let's end this blog before we derail from the main theme of the blog.

    Summary

    Before we leave let's summarize what we did so far.

    1. We created google maps places API.
    2. By installing the Flutter_DotEnv package, we secured the API from being public.
    3. HTTP package was also added which played a vital role in fetching temples lists from API.
    4. We created a utility file though with just one util. Later on, if you want, you can write a distance calculator method from user to temple to represent in google Maps.
    5. We then wrote a method in our provider class, that fetched a search list, and passes it to firebase cloud functions.
    6. The firebase function saves the temples lists to Firestore if the collection is empty.
    7. We then wrote an update trigger, that'll only run when the value is changed.

    Final Code

    temple_provider.dart

    import 'dart:convert';
import 'dart:math';
import 'package:cloud_firestore/cloud_firestore.dart';
import 'package:cloud_functions/cloud_functions.dart';
import 'package:firebase_auth/firebase_auth.dart';
import 'package:firebase_storage/firebase_storage.dart' as firbase_storage;
import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart';
import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart';
import 'package:http/http.dart' as http;

//custom modules
import 'package:temple/screens/temples/models/temple.dart';
import 'package:temple/screens/temples/utils/temple_utils.dart';

class TempleProvider with ChangeNotifier {
  // Instantiate FIbrebase products
  final FirebaseAuth auth = FirebaseAuth.instance;
  final FirebaseFunctions functions = FirebaseFunctions.instance;
  final FirebaseFirestore firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance;
  // Estabish sotrage instance for bucket of our choice
  // once e mulator runs you can find the bucket name at storage tab
  final firbase_storage.FirebaseStorage storage =
      firbase_storage.FirebaseStorage.instanceFor(
          bucket: 'astha-being-hindu-tutorial.appspot.com');

// Instantiate Temple Utils
  final TemplesUtils templesUtils = TemplesUtils();
  // Create the fake list of temples
  List<TempleModel>? _temples = [];
  // User location from db
  LatLng? _userLocation;

// Getters
  List<TempleModel> get temples => [..._temples as List];
  LatLng get userLocation => _userLocation as LatLng;

// List of Images
  static const List<String> imagePaths = [
    'image_1.jpg',
    'image_2.jpg',
    'image_3.jpg',
    'image_4.jpg',
  ];

// Future method to get temples
  Future<void> getNearyByTemples(LatLng userLocation) async {
    // Get urls from the temple utils
    Uri url = templesUtils.searchUrl(userLocation);

    try {
      // Set up references for firebase products.
      // Callable getNearbyTemples
      HttpsCallable getNearbyTemples =
          functions.httpsCallable('getNearbyTemples');
      // COllection reference for temples
      CollectionReference templeDocRef = firestore.collection('temples');
      // Get one doc from temples collection
      QuerySnapshot querySnapshot = await templeDocRef.limit(1).get();
      // A reference to a folder in storage that has images.
      firbase_storage.Reference storageRef = storage.ref('TempleImages');

      // We'll only get nearby temples if the temples collection empty
      if (querySnapshot.docs.isEmpty) {
        print("Temple collection is empty");
        // get the result from api search
        final res = await http.get(url);
        // decode the json result
        final decodedRes = await jsonDecode(res.body) as Map;
        // get result as list
        final results = await decodedRes['results'] as List;
        // Get random image url from available ones to put as images
        // Since we have 4 images we'll get 0-3 values from Random()
        final imgUrl = await storageRef
            .child(imagePaths[Random().nextInt(4)])
            .getDownloadURL();
        // Call the function
        final templesListCall = await getNearbyTemples.call(<String, dynamic>{
          'templeList': [...results],
          'imageRef': imgUrl,
        });

        // map the templesList restured by https callable
        final newTempleLists = templesListCall.data['temples']
            .map(
              (temple) => TempleModel(
                name: temple['name'],
                address: temple['address'],
                latLng: LatLng(
                  temple['latLng']['lat'],
                  temple['latLng']['lon'],
                ),
                imageUrl: temple['imageRef'],
                placesId: temple['place_id'],
              ),
            )
            .toList();
        // update the new temples list
        _temples = [...newTempleLists];
      } else {
        // If the temples collection already has temples then we won't write
        // but just fetch temples collection
        print("Temple collection is not empty");

        try {
          // get all temples documents
          final tempSnapShot = await templeDocRef.get();
          // fetch the values as list.
          final tempList = tempSnapShot.docs[0]['temples'] as List;
          // map the results into a list
          final templesList = tempList
              .map(
                (temple) => TempleModel(
                  name: temple['name'],
                  address: temple['address'],
                  latLng: LatLng(
                    temple['latLng']['lat'],
                    temple['latLng']['lon'],
                  ),
                  imageUrl: temple['imageRef'],
                  placesId: temple['place_id'],
                ),
              )
              .toList();
          // update temples
          _temples = [...templesList];
        } catch (e) {
          // incase of error temples list in empty
          _temples = [];
        }
      }
    } catch (e) {
      // incase of error temples list in empty

      _temples = [];
    }
// notify all the listeners
    notifyListeners();
  }
}

    index.js

    // Import modiules
const functions = require("firebase-functions"),
    admin = require('firebase-admin');

// always initialize admin 
admin.initializeApp();

// create a const to represent firestore
const db = admin.firestore();


// Create a new background trigger function 
exports.addTimeStampToUser = functions.runWith({
    timeoutSeconds: 240,  // Give timeout 
    memory: "512MB" // memory allotment 
}).firestore.document('users/{userId}').onCreate(async (_, context) => {
    // Get current timestamp from server
    let curTimeStamp = admin.firestore.Timestamp.now();
    // Print current timestamp on server
    functions.logger.log(`curTimeStamp ${curTimeStamp.seconds}`);

    try {
        // add the new value to new users document i
        await db.collection('users').doc(context.params.userId).set({ 'registeredAt': curTimeStamp, 'favTempleList': [], 'favShopsList': [], 'favEvents': [] }, { merge: true });
        // if its done print in logger
        functions.logger.log(`The current timestamp added to users collection:  ${curTimeStamp.seconds}`);
        // always return something to end the function execution
        return { 'status': 200 };
    } catch (e) {
        // Print error incase of errors
        functions.logger.log(`Something went wrong could not add timestamp to users collectoin ${curTimeStamp.seconds}`);
        // return status 400 for error
        return { 'status': 400 };
    }
});

// Create a function named addUserLocation 
exports.addUserLocation = functions.runWith({
    timeoutSeconds: 60,
    memory: "256MB"
}).https.onCall(async (data, context) => {

    try {
        // Fetch correct user document with user id.
        let snapshot = await db.collection('users').doc((context.auth.uid)).get();
        // Check if field value for location is null
        // functions.logger.log(snapshot['_fieldsProto']['userLocation']["valueType"] === "nullValue");
        let locationValueType = snapshot['_fieldsProto']['userLocation']["valueType"];
        if (locationValueType == 'nullValue') {
            await db.collection('users').doc((context.auth.uid)).set({ 'userLocation': data.userLocation }, { merge: true });
            functions.logger.log(`User location added    ${data.userLocation}`);
            return data.userLocation;

        }
        else {
            functions.logger.log(`User location not changed`);

        }

    }
    catch (e) {
        functions.logger.log(e);
        throw new functions.https.HttpsError('internal', e);
    }
    return data.userLocation;

});


exports.getNearbyTemples = functions.https.onCall(async (data, _) => {

    try {
        // Notify function's been called
        functions.logger.log("Add nearby temples function was called");
        // Create array of temple objects.
        let temples = data.templeList.map((temple) => {
            return {
                'place_id': temple['place_id'],
                'address': temple['vicinity'] ? temple['vicinity'] : 'Not Available',
                'name': temple['name'] ? temple['name'] : 'Not Available',
                'latLng': {
                    'lat': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lat'] : 'Not Available', 'lon': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lng'] : 'Not Available',
                    'dateAdded': admin.firestore.Timestamp.now()
                },
                'imageRef': data.imageRef
            }
        }

        );

        // save the temples array to temples collection as one document named temples 
        await db.collection('temples').add({ temples: temples });

    } catch (e) {
        // if error return errormsg
        return { 'Error Msg': e };
    }
    // If everything's fine return the temples array.
    return temples;
});


// When the temple List Updates
exports.updateNearbyTemples = functions.runWith({
    timeoutSeconds: 120,
    memory: "256MB"
}).firestore.document('temples/{id}').onUpdate(async (change, context) => {
    // If theres both new and old value
    if (change.before.exists && change.after.exists) {
        // temples list both new and old
        let newTemplesList = change.after.data()['temples'];
        let oldTemplesList = change.before.data()['temples'];

        // Places Id list from both new and old list
        let oldTemplesIdList = oldTemplesList.map(temple => temple['place_id']);
        let newTemplesIdList = newTemplesList.map(temple => temple['place_id']);

        // Lets find out if theres new temples id by filtering with old one
        let filteredList = newTemplesIdList.filter(x => !oldTemplesIdList.includes(x));

        // if the length are not same of fileted list has 
        //length of 0 then nothing new is there so just return
        if (oldTemplesIdList.length != newTemplesIdList.length || filteredList.length == 0) {
            functions.logger.log("Nothing is changed so onUpdate returned");
            return;
        }
        // If somethings changed then 
        try {
            functions.logger.log("On Update was called ");
            // Make new list of temples
            let temples = newTemplesList.map((temple) => {
                return {
                    'place_id': temple['place_id'],
                    'address': temple['vicinity'] ? temple['vicinity'] : 'Not Available',
                    'name': temple['name'] ? temple['name'] : 'Not Available',
                    'latLng': {
                        'lat': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lat'] : 'Not Available', 'lon': temple.hasOwnProperty('geometry') ? temple['geometry']['location']['lng'] : 'Not Available',
                        'dateAdded': admin.firestore.Timestamp.now()
                    }
                }
            }
            );
            // use the current context id to update temples, no need to merge
            await db.collection('temples').doc(context.params.id).set({ 'palces_id_list': newTemplesIdList, temples: temples });
        }
        catch (e) { throw e; }
        return { 'status': 200 };
    }
    // return nothing
    return null;
});

    Show Support

    Alright, this is it for this time. This series, is still not over, on the next upload we'll redesign our ugly-looking mundane homepage, create a Temple List Screen and execute the method we created today

    So, please do like, comment, and share the article with your friends. Thank you for your time and for those who are subscribing to the blog's newsletter, we appreciate it. Keep on supporting us. This is Nibesh from Khadka's Coding Lounge, a freelancing agency that makes websites and mobile applications.
item.author: kcl

item.title: 为您的旅程中断获取路边援助 | GET ROADSIDE ASSISTANCE FOR YOUR JOURNEY BREAKDOWN
item.description:
发表于无论是新车还是旧车，每个车主都有一个可怕的恐惧或噩梦，即他们的车辆会在长途旅行中或在城市中抛锚。 更糟糕的是，当你因为不知道该做什么或不知道该怎么做而无能为力时。 汽车故障不是笑话； 根据谨慎的数据，美国每年有三分之一的司机经历过车辆故障，除了带来麻烦和焦虑，每年给经济造成410亿美元的损失。 值得商榷的是，可以安全地假设故障过程非常耗时并且会掏空你的口袋。 即使您已经越过了汽车保养清单上的所有 I 和 T，并且偶尔进行频繁的汽车检查，在驾驶过程中仍有可能发生故障。 对于车龄超过 10 年的车主来说尤其如此。

这就是路边援助派上用场的地方。 路边援助被定义为在车辆发生故障后帮助您上路的汽车服务。 无论是爆胎、发动机爆裂、空油箱（您忘记加满油”）或快速启动，路边援助服务都能帮助您摆脱困境。 路边援助不仅适用于家用汽车，也适用于半卡车、车队、18 轮车等大型车辆。


服务：路边援助提供标准服务：爆胎服务：

适用于汽车和摩托车的爆胎。

拖车服务：
假设您的车辆出现发动机问题，除了将其拖到车库进行检查外，您无能为力。 在这种情况下，拖车服务涵盖将您的车辆拖到某些目的地的费用。

电池启动：
对于电池没电的汽车，路边援助很棒。

锁定服务：
在某些情况下，您将车钥匙留在车内并将自己锁在外面，在这种情况下，锁定服务可帮助您进入车内并取回钥匙。

绞盘服务：
有时，您在越野时会遇到泥潭或沟渠，您的车会被卡住，在这些情况下，路边援助很方便。

燃料中断：
即使您携带一瓶额外的燃料进行长途旅行，您也总是会用完。 那时路边援助会通过从最近的加油站带油来帮助您。

有几次，您为您的车辆购买了一项便宜的路边援助计划，您只能在几英里内使用路边援助，并且每年只能申请 4 次； 听起来很蹩脚。 但皇家行政服务路边援助可以为您提供路边援助服务的优惠，因为它们在 35 年内已涵盖约 20 亿美元的索赔。

假设您是一名商业司机，在半卡车、车队或卡车周围行驶，而您的车辆在携带沉重行李的途中坏了。 它不是典型的五人或七人车辆，可以通过一些推力将其放在一边。 您搜索我附近的商业道路救援或我附近的卡车道路救援但无济于事，因为没有大量公司提供全面的商业道路救援。 这就是 Encore 路边援助的用武之地。

他们的路边半卡车商业计划包括故障辅助、快速启动、爆胎更换、锁定辅助、燃料输送和碰撞辅助。

只需下载他们的应用程序并搜索我附近的卡车路边援助，您就会放心。

对于那些在我附近寻找 18 轮路边援助的人，请放心，您将在 Encore 广泛的保护范围内得到保障。

结论：
在这个时代和时代，解决问题是必须的； 这就是为什么通勤者想尽一切办法解决他们的汽车问题，但是如果他们被困在偏僻的地方，或者他们满载行李的半卡车轮胎瘪了，不能移动一英寸怎么办。 在这种情况下，路边援助就派上用场了，它可以解决您的问题，而不会让您的车辆干涸。 尽快上网并为您的车辆获得最佳路边援助。



原文:

Riteway Towing

Posted on Jun 3

Whether new or old, every car owner has a bad fear or nightmare that their vehicle will break down in the middle of a long trip or in the city. It is even worse when you can’t do anything about it just because you don’t know what to do or you don’t know how to do it. Car breakdowns are no laughing matter; according to cautious data, 1 in 3 drivers in America have experienced a breakdown of their vehicle every year, and besides carrying trouble and anxiety, causing the economy 41 billion dollars per annum. Debatably, it is safe to assume that the breakdown process is time-consuming and breaks your pocket. Even if you have crossed all the I’s and the T’s on your car maintenance list and do frequent car checks once in a while, there is still a chance of breakdown during the drive. This is especially true for owners of cars that are more than 10 years old.

This is where roadside assistance comes in handy. Roadside assistance is defined as the automobile service that helps you get on the road after a vehicle breakdown. Either if it’s a flat tire, blown engine, empty fuel tank (that you’ve forgotten to fill”), or a jump start, roadside assistance service will get you out of the tough spot. Roadside assistance is not just available to family cars but for big vehicles like semi-trucks, fleet, 18 wheelers as well.


Rite-Way Towing and Recovery provides affordable roadside assistance with a cheap roadside assistance rate, so your pockets are light that easily.

SERVICES: Roadside assistance provides standard services: Flat Tire Services:

For punctured tires in both cars and motorcycles.

Towing Services:
Suppose your vehicle is having engine problems, and you can’t do anything other than to tow it to a garage for a checkup. In that case, tow service coverage covers the cost of towing your vehicle to certain destinations.

Battery jumpstart:
For car that has dead batteries, roadside assistance is great.

Lockout service:
There are some instances where you leave your car keys inside and lock yourself outside, in which case lockout services help you get inside the car and retrieve the keys.

Winching services:
Sometimes, you are off-roading and come across a mud pool or a ditch where your car gets stuck, and roadside assistance is handy in these situations.

Fuel outage:
Even if you carry an extra bottle of fuel for a long journey, you can always run out of it. That is when roadside assistance helps you out by bringing oil from the nearest station.

On several occasions, you have purchased one of the cheap roadside assistance plans for your vehicle, where you can avail of roadside assistance for only a couple of miles and can only claim it 4 times a year; that sounds pretty lame. But Royal Administration service roadside assistance can get you good deals on roadside assistance services as they have covered claims of around $2 Billion in 35 years.

Suppose that you are a commercial driver and drive around semi-truck, fleet, or trucks, and your vehicle is broken while on the way with heavy luggage. It is not a typical five or 7-passenger vehicle which can be put to a side with some push. You search for commercial roadside assistance near me or truck roadside assistance near me but to no avail as there are not tons of companies providing commercial roadside assistance comprehensively. That is where Encore Roadside Assistance comes in.

Encore Protection provides semi-truck roadside assistance, fleet roadside assistance, and commercial truck roadside assistance.

Their roadside semi-truck commercial plans include breakdown assistance, jumpstarts, flat tire changes, lockout assistance, fuel delivery, and collision assistance.

The Encore’s truck roadside assistance and semi-roadside assistance program give you the freedom to drive to your destination without worrying that you might be stranded to nowhere and might not get help. Just download their app and search for truck roadside assistance near me, and you’ll be relieved.

Encore’s roadside assistance for semi-trucks will get special packages that include 25, 50, or even 100 towing miles, so no matter where you are, you’ll be towed to the nearest service stations without much hassle.

For those searching for 18-wheeler roadside assistance near me, rest assured that you’ll be covered inside the expansive coverage of Encore’s protection.

Conclusion:
In this age and era, solutions to problems are a must; that is why commuters try to bring every possible way and solution to their car problems, but what if they get stranded in the middle of nowhere or their semi-trucks full of luggage have flat tire and cannot move one inch. In this situation, roadside assistance comes handy, which covers your problems without leaving you dry with your vehicle. Go online and get the best roadside assistance for your vehicle as soon as possible.

item.title: 如何向用户显示它在您网站中的位置 | How to show the user it's location in your website
item.description:
不，我们不是在构建位置跟踪器来监视用户，但我们为什么不慷慨地向他们展示他们的住所呢？

我们可以利用开放层和以显示用户所在城市为焦点的地图。

的但我们将保持简单并假装我们跟踪我们的用户。

要使用这个 API，我们需要在 Geoapify 网站上注册为用户，他们有一个可用的免费层，它有足够的功能来构建我们的应用程序。 用适合自己的方式注册后，

  1. 转到仪表板
  2. 创建一个新项目并将其命名为您想要的任何名称
  3. 在左侧栏上，转到 API 密钥部分
  4. 选择应用程序类型作为 IP Geolocation API，因为这是我们将使用的 API
  5. 生成 API 密钥

在我们的 JavaScript 中，我们将使用 fetch 函数来获取一个 json 响应，我们可以使用它来收集我们需要的数据。

注意：在下面的 fetch 函数的 url 中替换没有你的实际钥匙

下面的代码有一个简单的 fetch 函数，带有对 REST API 的 GET 请求，即 IP Geolocation API

 fetch ( " https://api.geoapify.com/v1/ipinfo?&apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY " , requestOptions ) . then ( ( response ) => response . json () ) . then (( data ) => { console . log ( data ) }) . catch ( err => console . log ( err )) var requestOptions = { method : ' GET ' , };

请注意，现在我们只是在控制台中记录整个 .json 数据，但稍后我们将实际使用这些数据并将其放在地图上。
因此，如果您检查控制台，您应该会看到 API 带有一个具有很多属性的对象。

如果您想清楚地查看返回的 JSON 数据，请安装任何格式化程序浏览器扩展在 Web 浏览器中打开此链接，您将清楚地看到以可读方式很好地格式化的每个数据片段。
https://api.geoapify.com/v1/ipinfo?&apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY

现在我们得到这些数据后，我们需要把它放在地图上，我们将使用 OpenLayers 来简化我们的工作
如果您在此处看到快速入门指南。 他们向我们展示了一个带有 HTML 文件的工作示例。 我们需要使用 CDN 包含这个 JS 库，如下所示：

 <script src= "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/openlayers/openlayers.github.io@master/en/v6.14.1/build/ol.js" ></script> <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/openlayers/openlayers.github.io@master/en/v6.14.1/css/ol.css" type= "text/css" >

我们已经包含了 JS 库和它的样式表文件。

现在在我们的 JavaScript 文件中，我们可以像这样创建一个新地图：这正是快速入门指南中提到的内容

 var map = new ol . Map ({ target : ' map ' , layers : [ new ol . layer . Tile ({ source : new ol . source . OSM () }) ], view : new ol . View ({ center : ol . proj . fromLonLat ([ 37.41 , 8.82 ]), zoom : 4 }) });

请注意，此地图上位置的纬度和经度是硬编码作为 (37.41, 8.82) 这不是我们想要的。

让我们把上面的这段代码放在一个函数中，并称之为coordinates()

现在回到我们的 fetch 函数，而不是控制台记录我们的整个数据，让我们将所有这些数据传递给坐标函数，以便它可以被映射。

 fetch ( " https://api.geoapify.com/v1/ipinfo?&apiKey=18e9b1ba1eaf4731b0347ede6565a8a9 " , requestOptions ) . then ( ( response ) => response . json () ) . then (( data ) => { coordinates ( data ) // passing in the data }) . catch ( err => console . log ( err )) var requestOptions = { method : ' GET ' , };
 function coordinates ( data ) { // data is passed as a parameter var map = new ol . Map ({ target : ' map ' , layers : [ new ol . layer . Tile ({ source : new ol . source . OSM () }) ], view : new ol . View ({ center : ol . proj . fromLonLat ([ 37.41 , 8.82 ]), zoom : 12 }) }); }

现在用 Geoapify API 在检测到用户位置后返回的纬度和经度值替换硬编码的纬度和经度值。

在上图中，您可以看到 API 返回了许多我们可以使用的数据，但如果您仔细观察，它也会返回对象位置属性内的纬度和经度值要访问它，我们可以输入： 数据.位置.经度获取用户的经度。

所以现在在我们的坐标函数中，我们可以将硬编码的值替换为：

 function coordinates ( data ) { var map = new ol . Map ({ target : ' map ' , // div map is targeted in html layers : [ new ol . layer . Tile ({ source : new ol . source . OSM () }) ], view : new ol . View ({ center : ol . proj . fromLonLat ([ data . location . longitude , data . location . latitude ]), // hard coded values replaced zoom : 12 // how zoomed in the map is // the user can also change the zoom level of the map with the inbuilt buttons in the map by OpenLayers }) }); }

现在你可以看到我们的地图显示在具有 id 或类“地图”的 div 标记中所以你可以像这样创建它：

 <div id= "map" class= "map" ></div>

现在使用外部样式表文件向地图 div 添加一些基本 CSS

 .map { height : 650px ; width : 100% ; }

而现在每当任何用户打开你的网站，它都会通过这张地图显示用户的大概位置。

我继续在页面上以文本形式添加了其他详细信息，例如 IP 和国家/地区名称。

现场演示：这里

沃拉迪米尔·叶皮舍夫—— May 30 5月30日



原文:

No we are not building a location tracker to spy on the users , but why don't we generously show them where they live ?

We can make use of OpenLayers and Geoapify to display a map with the city of the user in focus .

OpenLayers is a JS library makes it easy to put a dynamic map in any web page , is completely free, Open Source JavaScript, released under the 2-clause BSD License (also known as the FreeBSD).

Geoapify API makes it extremely easy for us to find the user's location i.e. approximate latitude and longitude and the user's city , country and other info.

The The IP Geolocation API provided by Geoapify detects the user's location by IP address and then returns a .json file with all the details that could be found out from just the I.P. address of the user like it's country of residence , currency , phone code, postal code , language etc.This has some better use cases like determining which language the visitor speaks, which currency to use in payment forms and more data.
But we shall keep it simple and pretend that we track our users .

To use this API we need to register as a user on the Geoapify website , they have a free tier available which has enough feature to build our application . After registering with whatever method suits you ,

  1. Go to the Dashboard
  2. Create a new project and name it whatever you want
  3. On the left side bar , go to the API keys section
  4. Choose the type of App as IP Geolocation API as this is the API we will be using
  5. Generate a API key

Inside our JavaScript, we will use the fetch function to get a json response which we can use to gather the data we need .

Note : In the url of the fetch function below replace YOUR_API_KEY with out your actual key

The following code has a simple fetch function with the GET request to the REST API i.e. IP Geolocation API


fetch("https://api.geoapify.com/v1/ipinfo?&apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY", requestOptions) .then( (response) => response.json() ) .then((data) => { console.log(data) }) .catch(err => console.log(err)) var requestOptions = { method: 'GET',
};

Note that just for now we are just console logging the entire .json data in the console , but later we will actually use the data and put it on a map .
So if you check the console you should see that the API with an object with lot many properties.

If you want to view the return JSON data clearly , install any JSON formatter browser extension open this link in the web browser and you would clearly be able to see every data piece nicely formatted in a readable way .
https://api.geoapify.com/v1/ipinfo?&apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY

The API shows my current location with all the other details as you can see some of which I have blurred.

Now after we have got this data , we need to put this on a map and we will use OpenLayers to make our job easier
If you see the quick start guide here. They have shown us a working example with a HTML file . We need to include this JS library using CDN like so :

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/openlayers/openlayers.github.io@master/en/v6.14.1/build/ol.js"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet"href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/openlayers/openlayers.github.io@master/en/v6.14.1/css/ol.css" type="text/css">

We have include the JS library and it's stylesheet files as well.

Now inside our JavaScript file , we can create a new map like so: This is exactly what is mentioned in the Quick start guide

var map = new ol.Map({ target: 'map', layers: [ new ol.layer.Tile({ source: new ol.source.OSM() }) ], view: new ol.View({ center: ol.proj.fromLonLat([37.41, 8.82]), zoom: 4 }) });

Note that the latitude and longitude for the location on this map is harcoded as (37.41, 8.82) which is not what we want .

Let's put this piece of code above in a function and call it coordinates()

Now back to our fetch function instead of console logging our entire data , lets pass all this data to the coordinates function so that it can be mapped .


fetch("https://api.geoapify.com/v1/ipinfo?&apiKey=18e9b1ba1eaf4731b0347ede6565a8a9", requestOptions) .then( (response) => response.json() ) .then((data) => { coordinates(data) // passing in the data  }) .catch(err => console.log(err)) var requestOptions = { method: 'GET',
};

function coordinates(data) { // data is passed as a parameter  var map = new ol.Map({ target: 'map', layers: [ new ol.layer.Tile({ source: new ol.source.OSM() }) ], view: new ol.View({ center: ol.proj.fromLonLat([37.41, 8.82]), zoom: 12 }) });
}

Now to replace the hardcoded lat , long values with the latitude and longitude values returned by the Geoapify API after it detects the user location .

In the above image as you can see the API returned lots of data that we can use , but if you notice carefully it also returns the latitude and longitude values inside the location property of the object.

To access it we can type : data.location.longitude to get the user's longitude .

So now in our coordinates function we can replace the hardcoded values with :

function coordinates(data) { var map = new ol.Map({ target: 'map', // div map is targeted in html layers: [ new ol.layer.Tile({ source: new ol.source.OSM() }) ], view: new ol.View({ center: ol.proj.fromLonLat([data.location.longitude, data.location.latitude]), // hard coded values replaced  zoom: 12 // how zoomed in the map is 
// the user can also change the zoom level of the map with the inbuilt buttons in the map by OpenLayers }) });
}

Now as you can see our map is displayed in a div tag with id or class 'map' so you can create it like so :

<div id="map" class="map"></div>

Now add some basic CSS to the map div using external stylesheets file

.map { height: 650px; width: 100%;
}

And now whenever any user opens up your website , it will show the user's approximate location through this map .

I went ahead and added the other details like I.P. and country name in the form of text on the page.

Live Demo : here

Volodymyr Yepishev - May 30
item.author: satvik

item.title: 为了最好的开始......一个好的报价。 | For the best start... A good quote.
item.description:
米玛奇

发表于当行动变得无利可图时，收集信息； 当信息变得无利可图时，睡觉。
Ursula K. Le Guin, The Left Hand of Darkness Ursula K. Le Guin，黑暗的左手



原文:

mimateach

Posted on Jun 3

When action grows unprofitable, gather information; when information grows unprofitable, sleep.
Ursula K. Le Guin, The Left Hand of Darkness
item.author: mimateach

item.title: 使用 pg_hint_plan 和 pg_stat_statements 构建 PostgreSQL Docker 映像 | Build a PostgreSQL Docker image with pg_hint_plan and pg_stat_statements
item.description:
作为一名超过 20 年的数据库顾问，我 100% 相信任何数据库、实验室、开发人员或产品都应该有工具来解决和解决问题，这些工具已经安装好，可以使用了。 对于 PostgreSQL，这意味着两个扩展：pg_stat_statement 和 pg_hint_plan。 我永远不会明白为什么 pg_hint_plan 在所有安装中默认不存在。 通常，我会在实验室中重现这些问题，这里是如何构建一个安装了这两个镜像的 docker 镜像。

这是我从 PostgreSQL 版本 14 映像构建它的方法：

 cat > Dockerfile <<'DOCKERFILE' # install pg_hint_plan from rpm FROM docker.io/postgres:14 ADD https://github.com/ossc-db/pg_hint_plan/releases/download/REL14_1_4_0/pg_hint_plan14-1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm . RUN apt-get update -y ; apt-get install -y alien wget ; alien ./pg_hint_plan * .rpm ; dpkg -i pg-hint-plan * .deb # copy the minimal files to a postgres image FROM docker.io/postgres:14 COPY --from=0 /usr/pgsql-14/share/extension/pg_hint_plan.control /usr/share/postgresql/14/extension COPY --from=0 /usr/pgsql-14/share/extension/pg_hint_plan--1.4.sql /usr/share/postgresql/14/extension COPY --from=0 /usr/pgsql-14/lib/pg_hint_plan.so /usr/pgsql-14/lib/pg_hint_plan.so /usr/lib/postgresql/14/lib ENV PGPASSWORD=postgres CMD ["postgres","-c","shared_preload_libraries=pg_hint_plan,pg_stat_statements"] DOCKERFILE docker build -t pachot/pg_hint_plan --platform=linux/amd64 . docker push pachot/pg_hint_plan

并测试它：

 docker run -d --name pg -p 5432:5432 -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD = postgres pachot/pg_hint_plan sleep 5 docker exec -i pg psql -U postgres -e -c " create extension if not exists pg_hint_plan; create extension if not exists pg_stat_statements; create temporary table demo (a int primary key); " -c " explain select /*+ Leading((ab)) */ * from demo a join demo b using(a) " -c " explain select /*+ Leading((ba)) */ * from demo a join demo b using(a) " -c " select calls, query from pg_stat_statements; "

输出显示我的连接顺序遵循我使用领先提示设置的顺序，并且记录了所有语句的执行：

这对于排除故障和了解查询计划器的选择非常有帮助。 例如，在这里，我可以看到两个连接订单的成本是相同的，而一个是刚刚提取的，没有成本计算原因。

为了排除故障，您可以添加更多跟踪，例如：

 docker exec -i pg psql -U postgres -e -c " create temporary table demo (a int primary key); set pg_hint_plan.debug_print=verbose; set client_min_messages = log; " -c " explain select /*+ Leading((ba)) SeqScan(a) IndexScan(b) NestLoop(ab) SeqScan(x) IndexScan(a) Set(x=2) GoFaster(b) */ * from demo a join demo b using(a) "

使用set pg_hint_plan.debug_print=verbose和set client_min_messages = log会显示以下内容以了解使用了哪些提示：



原文:

With more than 20 years as a database consultant, I'm 100% convinced that any database, lab, dev, or prod, should have the tools to troubleshoot and workaround problems, already installed, ready to use. For PostgreSQL this means two extensions: pg_stat_statement and pg_hint_plan. I will never understand why pg_hint_plan is not there by default in all installations. Often, I reproduce the problems on a lab and here is how to build a docker image with both installed.

Here is how I build it from PostgreSQL version 14 image:


cat > Dockerfile <<'DOCKERFILE'
# install pg_hint_plan from rpm
FROM docker.io/postgres:14
ADD https://github.com/ossc-db/pg_hint_plan/releases/download/REL14_1_4_0/pg_hint_plan14-1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm .
RUN apt-get update -y ; apt-get install -y alien wget ; alien ./pg_hint_plan*.rpm ; dpkg -i pg-hint-plan*.deb
# copy the minimal files to a postgres image
FROM docker.io/postgres:14
COPY --from=0 /usr/pgsql-14/share/extension/pg_hint_plan.control /usr/share/postgresql/14/extension
COPY --from=0 /usr/pgsql-14/share/extension/pg_hint_plan--1.4.sql /usr/share/postgresql/14/extension
COPY --from=0 /usr/pgsql-14/lib/pg_hint_plan.so /usr/pgsql-14/lib/pg_hint_plan.so /usr/lib/postgresql/14/lib
ENV PGPASSWORD=postgres
CMD ["postgres","-c","shared_preload_libraries=pg_hint_plan,pg_stat_statements"]
DOCKERFILE

docker build -t pachot/pg_hint_plan --platform=linux/amd64 .
docker push pachot/pg_hint_plan

And test it:

docker run -d --name pg -p 5432:5432 -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres pachot/pg_hint_plan sleep 5
docker exec -i pg psql -U postgres -e -c "
create extension if not exists pg_hint_plan;
create extension if not exists pg_stat_statements;
create temporary table demo (a int primary key);
" -c "
explain select /*+ Leading((a b)) */ * from demo a join demo b using(a)
" -c "
explain select /*+ Leading((b a)) */ * from demo a join demo b using(a)
" -c "
select calls, query from pg_stat_statements;
"

The output shows that my join order follows what I've set with the Leading hint, and that all statements executions were recorded:

This is very helpful to troubleshoot and understand the choices of the query planner. For example, here, I can see that the cost of both join order are the same and one was just picked up with no costing reason.

And to troubleshoot the troubleshooting, you can add more traces like:

docker exec -i pg psql -U postgres -e -c "
create temporary table demo (a int primary key);
set pg_hint_plan.debug_print=verbose;
set client_min_messages = log;
" -c "
explain select /*+ Leading((b a)) SeqScan(a) IndexScan(b) NestLoop(a b) SeqScan(x) IndexScan(a) Set(x=2) GoFaster(b) */ * from demo a join demo b using(a)
"

With set pg_hint_plan.debug_print=verbose and set client_min_messages = log the following is displayed to understand which hints were used:
item.author: franckpachot

item.title: 想加入快速成长的卫星创业公司吗？ | Want to Join a Rapid Growth Satellite Startup?
item.description:
约翰加布里埃尔 Nguyen-Truong

发表于我在招聘。 认识任何有兴趣加入一家快速发展的卫星初创公司担任飞行软件工程师的人吗？ 想帮助开发最新的卫星技术吗？ 随时向我提出任何可能感兴趣的问题或推荐！

如果您有兴趣，我很乐意与您交谈并更好地了解您。 给我发 DM 或发送电子邮件至JohnGabriel.NguyenTruong@Igniterec.com 。

伟大的公司，快速增长并有机会获得股权。

需要至少 2-3 年以上的 C++ 经验，并且必须是美国公民或永久居民（美国政府空间技术出口法规）。

工资范围在105K-180K之间，具体取决于经验和能力。

在这里申请！



原文:
JohnGabriel Nguyen-Truong

Posted on Jun 3

I’m hiring. Know anyone who might be interested in joining a rapidly growing satellite startup company as a Flight Software Engineer? Want to help develop the latest satellite technologies? Feel free to shoot me any questions or referrals who might be interested!

If you are interested I'll love to talk and get to know you better. Shoot me a DM or email me at JohnGabriel.NguyenTruong@Igniterec.com.

Great company with rapid growth and a chance to earn equity.

Need at least 2-3+ years of C++ experience and must be US Citizen or permanent resident (U.S. Government space technology export regulations).

The salary range is between 105K-180K depending on experience and competency.

Apply Here!
